hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — literary/classical usage

jp_example: 鋭い洞察力を持っている。

cn_example_pinyin: Tā kāishǐ duòluò le.

jp_example: 他人の成功を嫉妬する。

jp_example: 業務を妨害する。

cn_meaning: To obstruct; to hinder; to impede
cn_example_pinyin: Fánghài gōngwù shì wéifǎ de.

jp_example: 潜在的な能力を引き出す。

jp_example: 顕著な成果を上げた。

cn_example_pinyin: Xiàoguǒ fēicháng xiǎnzhù.

cn_example_pinyin: Wǒ duì cǐ biǎoshì huáiyí.

jp_example: 秘密が暴露された。
jp_example_reading: ひみつがばくろされた。
jp_example_meaning: The secret was exposed.
cn_example_pinyin: Zhēnxiàng bèi bàolù le.

jp_example: 悲惨な事故が起きた。
jp_example_reading: ひさんなじこがおきた。
jp_example_meaning: A tragic accident occurred.
cn_example_pinyin: Qíngkuàng hěn bēicǎn.
cn_example_meaning: The situation is tragic.
freq_note: Common in news, literature, and storytelling

jp_example: 厳しい懲罰を受けた。
jp_example_reading: きびしいちょうばつをうけた。
jp_example_meaning: Received severe punishment.
cn_example_pinyin: Tā shòudào le chéngfá.
cn_example_meaning: He was punished.
freq_note: Common in education, legal, and parenting contexts

jp_example: 弁護士に相談する。

cn_meaning: To defend; to plead for; advocacy
cn_example_pinyin: Lǜshī wèi tā biànhù.
cn_example_meaning: The lawyer defended him.
freq_note: Common in legal and argumentative contexts

jp_example: 被害妄想に取りつかれる。

jp_example_meaning: To be gripped by persecution delusions.

cn_example_meaning: Don't delude yourself into thinking you can reach the top in one step.

jp_example: 覚悟を決める。
jp_example_reading: かくごをきめる。
jp_example_meaning: To steel one's resolve.
cn_example_pinyin: Tígāo zhèngzhì juéwù.
cn_example_meaning: Raise political consciousness.

freq_note: Common in political, educational, and moral contexts

jp_example: 皮肉な結果になった。
jp_example_reading: ひにくなけっかになった。

cn_example_pinyin: Pí ròu zhī kǔ.
cn_example_meaning: Physical suffering.

hsk_note: Individual characters are basic, but this compound is not an HSK vocabulary item

jp_example: 容疑者を拘束した。
jp_example_reading: ようぎしゃをこうそくした。
jp_example_meaning: The suspect was detained.
cn_meaning: Restrained; reserved; ill at ease; to constrain
cn_example_pinyin: Bùyào jūshù, suíbiàn zuò.
cn_example_meaning: Don't be reserved, sit anywhere you like.
meaning_shift_note: JP = physical/legal restraint or detention. CN = feeling reserved, shy, or uncomfortable in social situations.
freq_note: Common in social situations — said to guests to help them relax

jp_example: 他人の行動を模倣する。
jp_example_reading: たにんのこうどうをもほうする。
jp_example_meaning: To imitate others' behavior.
cn_meaning: To imitate; to copy; to mimic
cn_example_pinyin: Bùyào mángmù mófǎng.
cn_example_meaning: Don't blindly imitate.
freq_note: Common in education, art, and daily speech

jp_example_meaning: A financial panic broke out.
cn_example_pinyin: Bùyào kǒnghuāng.
cn_example_meaning: Don't panic.
freq_note: Common in news, economics, and crisis situations

jp_example: 目の錯覚だ。

jp_example_meaning: It's an optical illusion.
cn_example_pinyin: Zhè zhǐ shì yī zhǒng cuòjué.
cn_example_meaning: This is just an illusion.
freq_note: Common in psychology, art, and daily speech

jp_example: 妥当な判断だと思う。

cn_example_pinyin: Zhège ānpái hěn tuǒdāng.
cn_example_meaning: This arrangement is very proper.
freq_note: Common in formal and business contexts
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — related word 妥善 (tuǒshàn) is more common

jp_example: 厳密な調査を行う。
jp_example_reading: げんみつなちょうさをおこなう。
jp_example_meaning: To conduct a rigorous investigation.
cn_example_pinyin: Yánmì de luójí tuīlǐ.
cn_example_meaning: Rigorous logical reasoning.
freq_note: Common in academic, legal, and security contexts

jp_example: 勤勉な学生だ。
jp_example_reading: きんべんながくせいだ。
jp_example_meaning: They are a diligent student.
cn_example_pinyin: Tā shì yī gè qínmiǎn de rén.
cn_example_meaning: He is a diligent person.
freq_note: Formal/written — 勤奋 (qínfèn) is more common in speech

jp_example: 宗教的迫害を受ける。
jp_example_reading: しゅうきょうてきはくがいをうける。
jp_example_meaning: To suffer religious persecution.
cn_example_pinyin: Tā zāoshòu le zhèngzhì pòhài.
cn_example_meaning: He suffered political persecution.
freq_note: Common in historical, political, and human rights contexts

jp_example: 屈辱を受ける。
jp_example_reading: くつじょくをうける。
jp_example_meaning: To suffer humiliation.
cn_example_pinyin: Zhè shì yī zhǒng qūrǔ.
cn_example_meaning: This is a kind of humiliation.
freq_note: Common in historical and emotional contexts

jp_example: 華麗な衣装を身にまとう。
jp_example_reading: かれいないしょうをみにまとう。
jp_example_meaning: To wear gorgeous costumes.
cn_example_pinyin: Tā chuānzhe huálì de qúnzi.

cn_meaning: To flood; to overflow; to run rampant
cn_example_pinyin: Héshuǐ fànlàn chéng zāi.
cn_example_meaning: The river flooded and became a disaster.
freq_note: Common in news and metaphorical usage

jp_example: 貴族の没落を描いた小説。

jp_example_meaning: A novel depicting the downfall of the aristocracy.
cn_example_pinyin: Fēngjiàn shèhuì de mòluò.
cn_example_meaning: The decline of feudal society.
freq_note: Common in literary and historical contexts
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — literary/historical term

jp_example: 異質なものを排斥する。
jp_example_reading: いしつなものをはいせきする。
jp_example_meaning: To reject things that are different.
cn_meaning: To reject; to exclude; to repel
cn_example_pinyin: Bùyào páichì bùtóng de yìjiàn.
cn_example_meaning: Don't reject different opinions.
freq_note: Common in social, political, and scientific contexts

jp_example: 税金を免除される。
jp_example_reading: ぜいきんをめんじょされる。
jp_example_meaning: To be exempt from taxes.
cn_meaning: To exempt; to relieve; to waive
cn_example_pinyin: Miǎnchú zhàiwù.
cn_example_meaning: To waive the debt.
freq_note: Common in legal, financial, and policy contexts

jp_example: 解決策を模索する。

jp_example_meaning: To search for solutions.
cn_meaning: To grope; to fumble; to explore by trial
cn_example_pinyin: Wǒmen zài mōsuǒ qiánjìn.
cn_example_meaning: We are groping our way forward.
freq_note: Common in business, innovation, and problem-solving contexts

jp_example: 新しい文化が浸透する。
jp_example_reading: あたらしいぶんかがしんとうする。
jp_example_meaning: New culture permeates.
cn_example_pinyin: Yǔshuǐ shèntòu le qiángbì.

cn_example_pinyin: Yǐnbì zài shùcóng zhōng.
cn_example_meaning: Concealed in the bushes.

hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — medical/descriptive term

hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — scientific/technical term

hsk_note: Not part of HSK 1-6; used in political and legal contexts

hsk_note: Not part of HSK 1-6; frequent in international news

hsk_note: Not part of HSK 1-6; used in legal and interpersonal contexts

jp_example: 賃上げについて交渉する。

jp_example: 外交問題は慎重に扱うべきだ。

jp_example: 問題の深刻さを認識すべきだ。
jp_example_reading: もんだいのしんこくさをにんしきすべきだ。
jp_example_meaning: We should recognize the seriousness of the problem.
cn_meaning: To know (a person); to recognize; understanding
cn_example_pinyin: Nǐ rènshi tā ma?
cn_example_meaning: Do you know him?
meaning_shift_note: JP = abstract recognition/awareness of concepts. CN = primarily used for knowing/recognizing people, though it can also mean understanding in formal contexts.
freq_note: Extremely common — one of the most basic verbs in daily Mandarin

jp_example: 環境問題に対する意識が高まっている。
jp_example_reading: かんきょうもんだいにたいするいしきがたかまっている。
jp_example_meaning: Awareness of environmental issues is growing.
cn_example: 他意识到自己犯了一个错误。
cn_example_pinyin: Tā yìshí dào zìjǐ fàn le yī gè cuòwù.
cn_example_meaning: He realized that he had made a mistake.
freq_note: Very common in all registers — daily speech, news, academic writing

jp_example: 統計データに基づいて分析を行う。
jp_example_reading: とうけいデータにもとづいてぶんせきをおこなう。
jp_example_meaning: We conduct analysis based on statistical data.
cn_example_pinyin: Qǐng tǒngjì yīxià cānjiā rénshù.
cn_example_meaning: Please count the number of participants.
freq_note: Very common in business, academics, and government

jp_example: 原因を詳しく分析する。
jp_example_reading: げんいんをくわしくぶんせきする。
jp_example_meaning: To analyze the cause in detail.
cn_example: 我们需要仔细分析这个问题。
cn_example_pinyin: Wǒmen xūyào zǐxì fēnxī zhège wèntí.
cn_example_meaning: We need to carefully analyze this problem.
freq_note: Extremely common in all registers — academic, business, daily use

jp_example: 法律の解釈は専門家によって異なる。
jp_example_reading: ほうりつのかいしゃくはせんもんかによってことなる。
jp_example_meaning: The interpretation of the law differs among experts.
cn_example: 请你解释一下这个词的意思。
cn_example_pinyin: Qǐng nǐ jiěshì yīxià zhège cí de yìsi.
cn_example_meaning: Please explain the meaning of this word.
meaning_shift_note: JP = interpretation (often of texts, law, or meaning). CN = explanation in a broader sense, commonly used in daily life for explaining things.
freq_note: Extremely common — used daily in conversation and writing

jp_example: 環境への影響を考慮する必要がある。
jp_example_reading: かんきょうへのえいきょうをこうりょするひつようがある。
jp_example_meaning: It is necessary to consider the environmental impact.
cn_meaning: To consider; to think over; consideration
cn_example_pinyin: Ràng wǒ kǎolǜ yīxià.
cn_example_meaning: Let me think about it.
freq_note: Extremely common — used constantly in daily conversation

jp_example: 結果が出ないことに焦燥感を覚えた。
jp_example_reading: けっかがでないことにしょうそうかんをおぼえた。
jp_example_meaning: I felt frustration at the lack of results.
cn_example: 他变得越来越焦躁不安。
cn_example_pinyin: Tā biàn de yuè lái yuè jiāozào bù'ān.
cn_example_meaning: He became more and more restless and uneasy.
freq_note: Used in literary and formal contexts; 焦虑 is more common in daily speech
hsk_note: Not part of HSK 1-6; literary and formal vocabulary

jp_example: 彼は自分の成功に陶酔していた。
jp_example_reading: かれはじぶんのせいこうにとうすいしていた。
jp_example_meaning: He was intoxicated by his own success.
cn_meaning: To be intoxicated (figuratively); to be enraptured
cn_example: 她陶醉在美妙的音乐中。
cn_example_pinyin: Tā táozuì zài měimiào de yīnyuè zhōng.
cn_example_meaning: She was enraptured by the beautiful music.
freq_note: Common in literary and descriptive writing
hsk_note: Not part of HSK 1-6; literary and descriptive vocabulary

jp_example: 犯罪に対する憎悪の感情が湧いた。
jp_example_reading: はんざいにたいするぞうおのかんじょうがわいた。
jp_example_meaning: A feeling of hatred toward the crime welled up.
cn_example: 人们憎恶一切不公正的行为。
cn_example_pinyin: Rénmen zēngwù yīqiè bù gōngzhèng de xíngwéi.
cn_example_meaning: People loathe all unjust behavior.
freq_note: Used in formal and literary contexts; 恨 and 讨厌 are more common in daily speech
hsk_note: Not part of HSK 1-6; literary and formal vocabulary

jp_example: 彼は人生の苦悩を乗り越えた。
jp_example_reading: かれはじんせいのくのうをのりこえた。
jp_example_meaning: He overcame the anguish of life.
cn_example_pinyin: Zhè jiàn shì ràng tā hěn kǔnǎo.
cn_example_meaning: This matter made him very distressed.
meaning_shift_note: JP = deep existential anguish or suffering. CN = more commonly used for everyday worries or vexation, lighter in tone.
freq_note: Common in daily conversation and writing

jp_meaning: Deep emotion; strong feelings; being deeply moved
jp_example: 卒業式で感慨深い思いがした。
jp_example_reading: そつぎょうしきでかんがいぶかいおもいがした。
jp_example_meaning: I felt deeply moved at the graduation ceremony.
cn_meaning: Deep emotion; to sigh with feeling
cn_example: 他感慨万千地回忆起过去的日子。
cn_example_pinyin: Tā gǎnkǎi wànqiān de huíyì qǐ guòqù de rìzi.
cn_example_meaning: He recalled the past days with deep emotion.
freq_note: Common in literary and reflective writing; also used in conversation
hsk_note: Not part of HSK 1-6; literary and reflective vocabulary

jp_example: 異文化に対して寛容な態度が求められる。
jp_example_reading: いぶんかにたいしてかんようなたいどがもとめられる。
jp_example_meaning: A tolerant attitude toward other cultures is required.
cn_example_pinyin: Qǐng duì tā kuānróng yīxiē.
cn_example_meaning: Please be a little more tolerant of him.
freq_note: Common in interpersonal and moral discussions
hsk_note: Not part of HSK 1-6; used in moral and interpersonal discourse

jp_example: 彼の無礼な態度に激怒した。
jp_example_reading: かれのぶれいなたいどにげきどした。
jp_example_meaning: I was furious at his rude attitude.
cn_example_pinyin: Tā de huà jīnù le suǒyǒu rén.
cn_example_meaning: His words infuriated everyone.
freq_note: Common in narrative and news contexts
hsk_note: Not part of HSK 1-6; used in narrative and descriptive contexts

jp_example: 彼は過去の過ちに悔恨の念を抱いた。
jp_example_reading: かれはかこのあやまちにかいこんのねんをいだいた。
jp_example_meaning: He harbored feelings of remorse for his past mistakes.
cn_example: 他对自己的行为感到悔恨。
cn_example_pinyin: Tā duì zìjǐ de xíngwéi gǎndào huǐhèn.
cn_example_meaning: He felt deep remorse for his own behavior.
freq_note: Used in literary and formal contexts; 后悔 is more common in daily speech
hsk_note: Not part of HSK 1-6; literary and formal vocabulary

jp_example: 予想外の質問に困惑した。
jp_example_reading: よそうがいのしつもんにこんわくした。
jp_example_meaning: I was bewildered by the unexpected question.
cn_example: 这个问题让我感到困惑。
cn_example_pinyin: Zhège wèntí ràng wǒ gǎndào kùnhuò.
cn_example_meaning: This question left me feeling perplexed.
freq_note: Common in written and spoken Chinese

jp_example: 逆境に負けずに努力し続けた。
jp_example_reading: ぎゃっきょうにまけずにどりょくしつづけた。
jp_example_meaning: He kept making efforts without giving in to adversity.
cn_example: 在逆境中成长是一种宝贵的经历。
cn_example_pinyin: Zài nìjìng zhōng chéngzhǎng shì yī zhǒng bǎoguì de jīnglì.
cn_example_meaning: Growing in adversity is a valuable experience.
freq_note: Used in motivational, literary, and formal contexts; 困难 is more common
hsk_note: Not part of HSK 1-6; literary and motivational vocabulary

jp_example: 煩悩を捨て去ることは容易ではない。
jp_example_reading: ぼんのうをすてさることはよういではない。
jp_example_meaning: It is not easy to cast aside worldly desires.
cn_example_pinyin: Bié wèi zhè zhǒng xiǎo shì fánnǎo.
cn_example_meaning: Don't worry about such trivial matters.
meaning_shift_note: JP = Buddhist concept of worldly desires and passions that cause suffering. CN = everyday worry or trouble, much more colloquial and common.
freq_note: Extremely common in daily Mandarin for expressing worry or vexation

jp_example: 彼の才能には驚嘆するばかりだ。
jp_example_reading: かれのさいのうにはきょうたんするばかりだ。
jp_example_meaning: One can only marvel at his talent.
cn_meaning: To marvel; to exclaim in admiration
cn_example: 游客们对这里的美景惊叹不已。
cn_example_pinyin: Yóukèmen duì zhèlǐ de měijǐng jīngtàn bùyǐ.
cn_example_meaning: The tourists marveled endlessly at the beautiful scenery here.
freq_note: Common in descriptive and narrative writing
hsk_note: Not part of HSK 1-6; literary and descriptive vocabulary

jp_example: 秋の景色には哀愁が漂っている。
jp_example_reading: あきのけしきにはあいしゅうがただよっている。
jp_example_meaning: The autumn scenery is tinged with melancholy.
cn_example: 她的眼神中流露出淡淡的哀愁。
cn_example_pinyin: Tā de yǎnshén zhōng liúlù chū dàndàn de āichóu.
cn_example_meaning: A faint melancholy was revealed in her eyes.
freq_note: Used in literary and poetic contexts; 悲伤 is more common in everyday speech
hsk_note: Not part of HSK 1-6; literary and poetic vocabulary

jp_example: 愉快な時間を過ごした。
jp_example_reading: ゆかいなじかんをすごした。
jp_example_meaning: I spent a pleasant time.
cn_example: 祝你度过一个愉快的假期！
cn_example_pinyin: Zhù nǐ dùguò yī gè yúkuài de jiàqī!
cn_example_meaning: Wish you a pleasant holiday!
freq_note: Very common — used daily in greetings and well-wishes

jp_example: 戦争は人を狂気に駆り立てる。
jp_example_reading: せんそうはひとをきょうきにかりたてる。
jp_example_meaning: War drives people to madness.
cn_example_pinyin: Rénmen fēngkuáng de qiǎnggòu shāngpǐn.
cn_example_meaning: People frantically rushed to buy goods.
meaning_shift_note: JP = clinical or literary insanity/madness. CN = broader usage including 'crazy' in casual contexts (crazy sales, crazy fans), much more colloquial.
freq_note: Very common in daily speech, often used figuratively

jp_example: 過酷な労働条件に耐えている。
jp_example_reading: かこくなろうどうじょうけんにたえている。
jp_example_meaning: They are enduring harsh working conditions.
cn_example_pinyin: Zhànzhēng de xiànshí shì yánkù de.
cn_example_meaning: The reality of war is harsh.
freq_note: Used in formal and literary contexts; 残酷 is more commonly used
hsk_note: Not part of HSK 1-6; literary and formal vocabulary

jp_example: 卑劣な手段を使ってはいけない。
jp_example_reading: ひれつなしゅだんをつかってはいけない。
jp_example_meaning: You must not use despicable means.
cn_example: 这种卑劣的行为令人不齿。
cn_example_pinyin: Zhè zhǒng bēiliè de xíngwéi lìng rén bùchǐ.
cn_example_meaning: This kind of despicable behavior is contemptible.
freq_note: Used in formal and literary contexts for moral condemnation
hsk_note: Not part of HSK 1-6; literary and formal vocabulary

jp_example: 崇高な理想を追い求める。
jp_example_reading: すうこうなりそうをおいもとめる。
jp_example_meaning: To pursue lofty ideals.
cn_example_pinyin: Tā huáiyǒu chónggāo de lǐxiǎng.
cn_example_meaning: He harbors lofty ideals.
freq_note: Used in formal, literary, and political contexts
hsk_note: Not part of HSK 1-6; formal and literary vocabulary

jp_example: 巧妙な手口で犯罪が行われた。
jp_example_reading: こうみょうなてぐちではんざいがおこなわれた。
jp_example_meaning: The crime was carried out with clever methods.
cn_example: 他想出了一个巧妙的办法。
cn_example_pinyin: Tā xiǎng chū le yī gè qiǎomiào de bànfǎ.
cn_example_meaning: He came up with an ingenious solution.
freq_note: Common in descriptive writing and everyday speech
hsk_note: Not part of HSK 1-6; used in descriptive and narrative contexts

jp_example: 精密な検査を行う必要がある。
jp_example_reading: せいみつなけんさをおこなうひつようがある。
jp_example_meaning: It is necessary to conduct a detailed examination.
cn_example: 这台精密仪器非常昂贵。
cn_example_pinyin: Zhè tái jīngmì yíqì fēicháng ángguì.
cn_example_meaning: This precision instrument is very expensive.
freq_note: Common in technical, scientific, and manufacturing contexts
hsk_note: Not part of HSK 1-6; technical and scientific vocabulary

jp_example: 鮮明な記憶が蘇ってきた。
jp_example_reading: せんめいなきおくがよみがえってきた。
jp_example_meaning: Vivid memories came back to me.
cn_example: 这幅画的色彩非常鲜明。
cn_example_pinyin: Zhè fú huà de sècǎi fēicháng xiānmíng.
cn_example_meaning: The colors of this painting are very vivid.
freq_note: Common in descriptive writing and news
hsk_note: Not part of HSK 1-6; used in descriptive and analytical writing

jp_example: 環境汚染は深刻な問題だ。
jp_example_reading: かんきょうおせんはしんこくなもんだいだ。
jp_example_meaning: Environmental pollution is a serious problem.
cn_example: 这部电影给我留下了深刻的印象。
cn_example_pinyin: Zhè bù diànyǐng gěi wǒ liúxià le shēnkè de yìnxiàng.
cn_example_meaning: This movie left a deep impression on me.
meaning_shift_note: JP = serious/grave (a serious problem). CN = profound/deep (a deep impression). The nuance differs significantly.
freq_note: Very common in all registers — daily speech, news, academic

jp_example: 住民の切実な声に耳を傾けるべきだ。
jp_example_reading: じゅうみんのせつじつなこえにみみをかたむけるべきだ。
jp_example_meaning: We should listen to the earnest voices of the residents.
cn_example: 我们要切实解决群众的困难。
cn_example_pinyin: Wǒmen yào qièshí jiějué qúnzhòng de kùnnán.
cn_example_meaning: We must earnestly solve the difficulties of the masses.
meaning_shift_note: JP = urgent, keenly felt, acute (emotional urgency). CN = practical, realistic, conscientious (emphasis on doing things properly).
freq_note: Common in formal and political speech
hsk_note: Not part of HSK 1-6; formal and political vocabulary

jp_example: 山頂からの眺めは壮観だった。
jp_example_reading: さんちょうからのながめはそうかんだった。
jp_example_meaning: The view from the mountaintop was spectacular.
cn_example_pinyin: Chángchéng fēicháng zhuàngguān.
cn_example_meaning: The Great Wall is very spectacular.
freq_note: Common in travel, descriptive, and news contexts
hsk_note: Not part of HSK 1-6; descriptive vocabulary for scenery and events

jp_example: 将来に対して漠然とした不安を感じる。
jp_example_reading: しょうらいにたいしてばくぜんとしたふあんをかんじる。
jp_example_meaning: I feel a vague anxiety about the future.
cn_example_pinyin: Tā duì zhè jiàn shì mòrán zhì zhī.
cn_example_meaning: He treated this matter with indifference.
meaning_shift_note: JP = vague, hazy, unclear. CN = indifferent, apathetic, unconcerned. The meanings are quite different.
freq_note: Used in literary and formal contexts
hsk_note: Not part of HSK 1-6; literary vocabulary

jp_example: 彼の説明は明瞭で分かりやすかった。
jp_example_reading: かれのせつめいはめいりょうでわかりやすかった。
jp_example_meaning: His explanation was clear and easy to understand.
cn_example_pinyin: Zhège dàolǐ jiǎndān míngliǎo.
cn_example_meaning: This principle is simple and clear.
freq_note: Common in written Chinese and formal contexts
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — related word 明白 (míngbai) is HSK 3

jp_example: 彼は莫大な財産を築いた。
jp_example_reading: かれはばくだいなざいさんをきずいた。
jp_example_meaning: He amassed an enormous fortune.
cn_example: 他为公司做出了莫大的贡献。
cn_example_pinyin: Tā wèi gōngsī zuòchūle mò dà de gòngxiàn.
cn_example_meaning: He made an immense contribution to the company.
freq_note: Used in formal/literary Chinese — less common in casual speech
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — literary/formal usage

jp_example: 彼は政府を痛烈に批判した。
jp_example_reading: かれはせいふをつうれつにひはんした。
jp_example_meaning: He scathingly criticized the government.
cn_meaning: Bitter; severe; intense (of criticism or pain)
cn_example: 他对腐败现象进行了痛烈的批判。
cn_example_pinyin: Tā duì fǔbài xiànxiàng jìnxíngle tòngliè de pīpàn.
cn_example_meaning: He bitterly criticized the corruption.
freq_note: Somewhat literary — 猛烈 or 激烈 are more common alternatives in CN
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — literary register

jp_example: 水が蒸発して空気中に消えた。
jp_example_reading: みずがじょうはつしてくうきちゅうにきえた。
jp_example_meaning: The water evaporated and disappeared into the air.
cn_example_pinyin: Gāowēn shǐ shuǐfèn xùnsù zhēngfā.
cn_example_meaning: High temperature causes moisture to evaporate rapidly.
freq_note: Common in science, daily life, and news contexts

jp_example: 金属が腐食して使えなくなった。
jp_example_reading: きんぞくがふしょくしてつかえなくなった。
jp_example_meaning: The metal corroded and became unusable.
cn_example_pinyin: Suānxìng yètǐ huì fǔshí jīnshǔ.
cn_example_meaning: Acidic liquids can corrode metal.
freq_note: Common in science, engineering, and figurative use (corrupting influence)
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — technical/scientific vocabulary

jp_example: この作品は長年の努力の結晶だ。
jp_example_reading: このさくひんはながねんのどりょくのけっしょうだ。
jp_example_meaning: This work is the crystallization of years of effort.
cn_example: 这项发明是团队智慧的结晶。
cn_example_pinyin: Zhè xiàng fāmíng shì tuánduì zhìhuì de jiéjīng.
cn_example_meaning: This invention is the crystallization of the team's wisdom.
freq_note: Common in both scientific and figurative contexts
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — used in science and formal writing

jp_example: 不動産投機でかなりの利益を得た。
jp_example_reading: ふどうさんとうきでかなりのりえきをえた。
jp_example_meaning: He made considerable profit from real estate speculation.
cn_example: 投机行为扰乱了市场秩序。
cn_example_pinyin: Tóujī xíngwéi rǎoluànle shìchǎng zhìxù.
cn_example_meaning: Speculative behavior disrupted market order.
freq_note: Common in financial news and economic discussions
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — financial/business vocabulary

jp_example: その企業は市場を独占している。
jp_example_reading: そのきぎょうはしじょうをどくせんしている。
jp_example_meaning: That company monopolizes the market.
cn_meaning: Monopoly; to monopolize; to exclusively occupy
cn_example_pinyin: Tā xiǎng dúzhàn suǒyǒu de zīyuán.
cn_example_meaning: He wants to monopolize all the resources.
freq_note: Common in business, economics, and daily contexts — 垄断 is more formal
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — 垄断 (lǒngduàn) is the more formal equivalent

jp_example: その会社は経営破綻に陥った。
jp_example_reading: そのかいしゃはけいえいはたんにおちいった。
jp_example_meaning: That company fell into financial collapse.
cn_example: 他的谎言终于露出了破绽。
cn_example_pinyin: Tā de huǎngyán zhōngyú lùchūle pòzhàn.
cn_example_meaning: His lies finally revealed a flaw.
meaning_shift_note: JP = bankruptcy/collapse/failure. CN 破绽 = flaw/weak point/loophole — completely different meanings despite shared characters.
freq_note: Common in daily speech and literature — often used for finding flaws in arguments or lies
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — colloquial but widely used

jp_example: 海外への投資が増えている。
jp_example_reading: かいがいへのとうしがふえている。
jp_example_meaning: Investment abroad is increasing.
cn_example: 他决定投资一家新公司。
cn_example_pinyin: Tā juédìng tóuzī yī jiā xīn gōngsī.
cn_example_meaning: He decided to invest in a new company.
freq_note: Extremely common — used in business, news, and daily conversation

jp_example: 不況の影響で多くの企業が倒産した。
jp_example_reading: ふきょうのえいきょうでおおくのきぎょうがとうさんした。
jp_example_meaning: Many companies went bankrupt due to the recession.
cn_meaning: Bankruptcy; to go bankrupt; to close down
cn_example: 经济危机导致很多企业倒闭。
cn_example_pinyin: Jīngjì wēijī dǎozhì hěn duō qǐyè dǎobì.
cn_example_meaning: The economic crisis caused many businesses to go bankrupt.
freq_note: Very common in news and business — 倒闭 is the standard CN equivalent

jp_example: 上司はその行為を黙認した。
jp_example_reading: じょうしはそのこういをもくにんした。
jp_example_meaning: The boss tacitly approved that behavior.
cn_meaning: Default (computing); tacit approval; to accept silently
cn_example: 系统的默认设置需要修改。
cn_example_pinyin: Xìtǒng de mòrèn shèzhì xūyào xiūgǎi.
cn_example_meaning: The system's default settings need to be changed.
meaning_shift_note: JP = tacit approval/silent consent. CN 默认 is now predominantly used to mean 'default' (computing/settings), though the original meaning of tacit approval also exists.
freq_note: Extremely common in modern Chinese — especially in tech/computing contexts
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — but widely used in modern Chinese especially in tech

jp_example: 彼女は申し出を拒絶した。
jp_example_reading: かのじょはもうしでをきょぜつした。
jp_example_meaning: She rejected the offer.
cn_meaning: To refuse; to reject; to decline
cn_example_pinyin: Tā jùjuéle tā de yāoqǐng.
cn_example_meaning: She rejected his invitation.
freq_note: Extremely common — used in daily conversation across all registers

jp_example: 政府はその法案を撤回した。
jp_example_reading: せいふはそのほうあんをてっかいした。
jp_example_meaning: The government withdrew the bill.
cn_meaning: To withdraw; to retract; to revoke
cn_example_pinyin: Tā chèhuíle zhīqián de fāyán.
cn_example_meaning: He retracted his previous statement.
freq_note: Common in news, legal, and formal contexts
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — formal/legal register

jp_example: 交渉でお互いに譲歩する必要がある。
jp_example_reading: こうしょうでおたがいにじょうほするひつようがある。
jp_example_meaning: We need to make concessions in the negotiations.
cn_meaning: To make a concession; to yield; to give in
cn_example_pinyin: Shuāngfāng dōu bù yuànyì ràngbù.
cn_example_meaning: Neither side is willing to make concessions.
freq_note: Common in negotiations, diplomacy, and daily discussions

jp_example: 政府が市場に介入した。
jp_example_reading: せいふがしじょうにかいにゅうした。
jp_example_meaning: The government intervened in the market.
cn_example_pinyin: Jǐngfāng jièrùle zhè qǐ jiūfēn.
cn_example_meaning: The police intervened in this dispute.
freq_note: Very common in news, politics, and daily contexts

jp_example: 道路が遮断されて通行できない。
jp_example_reading: どうろがしゃだんされてつうこうできない。
jp_example_meaning: The road was blocked and traffic cannot pass.
cn_meaning: To block; to cut off; to intercept
cn_example: 大雪遮断了通往山区的道路。
cn_example_pinyin: Dà xuě zhēduànle tōngwǎng shānqū de dàolù.
cn_example_meaning: Heavy snow blocked the roads leading to the mountain area.
freq_note: Less common in CN — 阻断 or 切断 are more frequently used alternatives
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — 切断 (qiēduàn) is a more common alternative

jp_example: 不審者が敷地内に侵入した。
jp_example_reading: ふしんしゃがしきちないにしんにゅうした。
jp_example_meaning: A suspicious person intruded onto the premises.
cn_meaning: To invade; to intrude; to infiltrate
cn_example: 黑客侵入了公司的系统。
cn_example_pinyin: Hēikè qīnrùle gōngsī de xìtǒng.
cn_example_meaning: Hackers infiltrated the company's system.
freq_note: Common in news, security, and technology contexts
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — related word 侵略 (qīnlüè) is HSK 6

jp_example: 軍隊は撤退を余儀なくされた。
jp_example_reading: ぐんたいはてったいをよぎなくされた。
jp_example_meaning: The troops were forced to retreat.
cn_meaning: To retreat; to withdraw; to pull back
cn_example_pinyin: Bùduì yǐjīng cóng qiánxiàn chètuì.
cn_example_meaning: The troops have already withdrawn from the front line.
freq_note: Common in military, news, and business contexts (market withdrawal)
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — military/formal vocabulary

jp_example: 警察は現場を封鎖した。
jp_example_reading: けいさつはげんばをふうさした。
jp_example_meaning: The police sealed off the scene.
cn_meaning: Blockade; lockdown; to seal off; to block (information)
cn_example_pinyin: Xiāoxi bèi yángé fēngsuǒ le.
cn_example_meaning: The news was strictly blocked.
freq_note: Very common — especially in news, politics, and pandemic contexts

jp_example: 防犯カメラで24時間監視している。
jp_example_reading: ぼうはんカメラでにじゅうよじかんかんししている。
jp_example_meaning: We monitor 24 hours with security cameras.
cn_meaning: To monitor; to keep watch; surveillance
cn_example: 警方一直在监视嫌疑人的行动。
cn_example_pinyin: Jǐngfāng yīzhí zài jiānshì xiányírén de xíngdòng.
cn_example_meaning: The police have been monitoring the suspect's movements.
freq_note: Very common in news, security, and technology contexts
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — related word 监督 (jiāndū) is HSK 6

jp_example: 容疑者は48時間拘留された。
jp_example_reading: ようぎしゃはよんじゅうはちじかんこうりゅうされた。
jp_example_meaning: The suspect was detained for 48 hours.
cn_meaning: Detention; to detain; to hold in custody
cn_example: 他因违法行为被拘留了十天。
cn_example_pinyin: Tā yīn wéifǎ xíngwéi bèi jūliúle shí tiān.
cn_example_meaning: He was detained for ten days for illegal behavior.
freq_note: Common in legal and news contexts
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — legal vocabulary

jp_example: 警察は容疑者を尋問した。
jp_example_reading: けいさつはようぎしゃをじんもんした。
jp_example_meaning: The police interrogated the suspect.
cn_meaning: To interrogate; to question (in legal context)
cn_example: 法官对被告进行了讯问。
cn_example_pinyin: Fǎguān duì bèigào jìnxíngle xùnwèn.
cn_example_meaning: The judge interrogated the defendant.
freq_note: Common in legal and news contexts — 审问 is another common synonym
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — legal/formal vocabulary

jp_example: 内部の不正を告発した。
jp_example_reading: ないぶのふせいをこくはつした。
jp_example_meaning: He reported internal misconduct.
cn_meaning: To report (a crime); to inform against; to accuse
cn_example: 他向警方告发了犯罪行为。
cn_example_pinyin: Tā xiàng jǐngfāng gàofāle fànzuì xíngwéi.
cn_example_meaning: He reported the criminal activity to the police.
freq_note: Used in legal contexts — 举报 (jǔbào) is more common in modern Chinese
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — legal vocabulary; 举报 is more commonly used

jp_example: 容疑者は証拠不十分で釈放された。
jp_example_reading: ようぎしゃはしょうこふじゅうぶんでしゃくほうされた。
jp_example_meaning: The suspect was released due to insufficient evidence.
cn_meaning: To release; to set free; to emit (energy/signals)
cn_example_pinyin: Fǎyuàn juédìng shìfàng bèigào.
cn_example_meaning: The court decided to release the defendant.
freq_note: Very common — used in legal, scientific, and figurative contexts

jp_example: 彼は脅迫を受けて金を払った。
jp_example_reading: かれはきょうはくをうけてかねをはらった。
jp_example_meaning: He paid money after being threatened.
cn_example_pinyin: Tā bèi xiépò qiānshǔle wénjiàn.
cn_example_meaning: He was coerced into signing the document.
freq_note: Common in legal and news contexts
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — legal/formal vocabulary

jp_example: 違反者は厳しく処罰される。
jp_example_reading: いはんしゃはきびしくしょばつされる。
jp_example_meaning: Violators will be severely punished.
cn_example: 违规者将受到严厉处罚。
cn_example_pinyin: Wéiguī zhě jiāng shòudào yánlì chǔfá.
cn_example_meaning: Those who violate the rules will be severely punished.
freq_note: Very common — used in legal, educational, and daily contexts
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — related word 惩罚 (chéngfá) is HSK 6

jp_example: 違法な薬物が没収された。
jp_example_reading: いほうなやくぶつがぼっしゅうされた。
jp_example_meaning: The illegal drugs were confiscated.
cn_example: 海关没收了大量走私物品。
cn_example_pinyin: Hǎiguān mòshōule dàliàng zǒusī wùpǐn.
cn_example_meaning: Customs confiscated a large amount of smuggled goods.
freq_note: Common in legal and news contexts
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — legal/news vocabulary

jp_example: 彼は組織から追放された。
jp_example_reading: かれはそしきからついほうされた。
jp_example_meaning: He was expelled from the organization.
cn_meaning: To expel; to drive out; to deport
cn_example: 非法入境者将被驱逐出境。
cn_example_pinyin: Fēifǎ rùjìng zhě jiāng bèi qūzhú chūjìng.
cn_example_meaning: Illegal immigrants will be deported.
freq_note: Common in legal, political, and news contexts — 驱逐 is the standard CN equivalent
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — CN equivalent 驱逐 is formal/legal vocabulary

jp_example: 野党は政府の方針を糾弾した。
jp_example_reading: やとうはせいふのほうしんをきゅうだんした。
jp_example_meaning: The opposition party denounced the government's policy.
cn_example: 古代御史有纠弹百官的权力。
cn_example_pinyin: Gǔdài yùshǐ yǒu jiūtán bǎiguān de quánlì.
cn_example_meaning: In ancient times, censors had the power to impeach officials.
meaning_shift_note: JP uses 糾弾 actively in modern political discourse. CN 纠弹 is archaic/classical — modern CN would use 谴责 (qiǎnzé) or 弹劾 (tánhé) instead.
freq_note: Archaic in modern Chinese — 谴责 or 弹劾 are the modern equivalents
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — archaic/classical Chinese term

jp_example: 長引く不況で地方経済が疲弊している。
jp_example_reading: ながびくふきょうでちほうけいざいがひへいしている。
jp_example_meaning: The local economy is exhausted due to the prolonged recession.
cn_meaning: Exhausted; worn out; tired and weakened
cn_example: 连年战争使国家疲敝不堪。
cn_example_pinyin: Liánnián zhànzhēng shǐ guójiā píbì bùkān.
cn_example_meaning: Years of war left the country utterly exhausted.
freq_note: Literary/formal — 疲惫 (píbèi) is much more common in modern CN
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — literary register; 疲惫 (píbèi) is HSK 6

jp_example: そのニュースは社会に大きな衝撃を与えた。
jp_example_reading: そのニュースはしゃかいにおおきなしょうげきをあたえた。
jp_example_meaning: The news gave a major shock to society.
cn_meaning: Impact; shock; to assault; to charge
cn_example: 这件事对他造成了巨大的冲击。
cn_example_pinyin: Zhè jiàn shì duì tā zàochéngle jùdà de chōngjī.
cn_example_meaning: This incident caused a huge shock to him.
freq_note: Very common — used in news, daily life, and figurative contexts

jp_example: 監督は選手たちを激励した。
jp_example_reading: かんとくはせんしゅたちをげきれいした。
jp_example_meaning: The coach encouraged the players.
cn_meaning: To encourage; to inspire; to motivate
cn_example: 老师的话激励了我努力学习。
cn_example_pinyin: Lǎoshī de huà jīlìle wǒ nǔlì xuéxí.
cn_example_meaning: The teacher's words inspired me to study hard.
freq_note: Very common — widely used in education, business, and daily life

jp_example: 彼の成功は多くの人を鼓舞した。
jp_example_reading: かれのせいこうはおおくのひとをこぶした。
jp_example_meaning: His success inspired many people.
cn_meaning: To inspire; to encourage; to uplift
cn_example: 这场胜利极大地鼓舞了士气。
cn_example_pinyin: Zhè chǎng shènglì jídà de gǔwǔle shìqì.
cn_example_meaning: This victory greatly boosted morale.
freq_note: Very common — used in news, speeches, and daily life

jp_example: 政府は省エネを奨励している。
jp_example_reading: せいふはしょうエネをしょうれいしている。
jp_example_meaning: The government is promoting energy conservation.
cn_meaning: Reward; incentive; to reward; to encourage
cn_example: 公司奖励了表现优秀的员工。
cn_example_pinyin: Gōngsī jiǎnglìle biǎoxiàn yōuxiù de yuángōng.
cn_example_meaning: The company rewarded employees who performed well.
meaning_shift_note: JP 奨励 emphasizes encouragement/promotion of behavior. CN 奖励 emphasizes reward/prize — it is more about giving tangible rewards than simply encouraging.
freq_note: Very common — used in education, business, and daily contexts

jp_example: 甘い誘惑に負けてしまった。
jp_example_reading: あまいゆうわくにまけてしまった。
jp_example_meaning: I gave in to sweet temptation.
cn_meaning: Temptation; to tempt; to lure; to seduce
cn_example: 他抵挡不住金钱的诱惑。
cn_example_pinyin: Tā dǐdǎng bù zhù jīnqián de yòuhuò.
cn_example_meaning: He couldn't resist the temptation of money.
freq_note: Very common — used across all registers in daily life

jp_example: 安全対策を軽視してはいけない。
jp_example_reading: あんぜんたいさくをけいししてはいけない。
jp_example_meaning: You must not disregard safety measures.
cn_meaning: To look down on; to despise; to underestimate
cn_example: 不要轻视任何一个对手。
cn_example_pinyin: Bùyào qīngshì rènhé yī gè duìshǒu.
cn_example_meaning: Don't underestimate any opponent.
freq_note: Common in formal and daily contexts
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — related word 重视 (zhòngshì) is HSK 5

jp_example: 怠惰な生活を送ってはいけない。
jp_example_reading: たいだなせいかつをおくってはいけない。
jp_example_meaning: You shouldn't lead a lazy life.
cn_example: 怠惰的人很难取得成功。
cn_example_pinyin: Dàiduò de rén hěn nán qǔdé chénggōng.
cn_example_meaning: Lazy people can hardly achieve success.
freq_note: Somewhat literary — 懒惰 (lǎnduò) is much more common in daily speech
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — 懒 (lǎn) is HSK 5; 懒惰 is more common

jp_example: 彼女は勇敢に困難に立ち向かった。
jp_example_reading: かのじょはゆうかんにこんなんにたちむかった。
jp_example_meaning: She bravely faced the difficulties.
cn_example: 消防员勇敢地冲进了火场。
cn_example_pinyin: Xiāofángyuán yǒnggǎn de chōngjìnle huǒchǎng.
cn_example_meaning: The firefighter bravely rushed into the fire.
freq_note: Very common — used across all registers in daily life

jp_example: 彼は会社に忠実な社員だ。
jp_example_reading: かれはかいしゃにちゅうじつなしゃいんだ。
jp_example_meaning: He is a loyal employee to the company.
cn_example_pinyin: Tā shì yī gè zhōngshí de péngyǒu.
cn_example_meaning: He is a faithful friend.
freq_note: Very common — used in daily life, literature, and formal contexts

jp_example: 彼はどんなに成功しても謙虚な姿勢を崩さない。
jp_example_reading: かれはどんなにせいこうしてもけんきょなしせいをくずさない。
jp_example_meaning: No matter how successful he is, he never loses his humble attitude.
cn_example_pinyin: Zuòrén yào qiānxū, bùyào jiāo'ào.
cn_example_meaning: Be humble, don't be arrogant.
freq_note: Very common — a core virtue in Chinese culture, used daily

jp_example: 彼女は素直に謝った。
jp_example_reading: かのじょはすなおにあやまった。
jp_example_meaning: She honestly apologized.
cn_example: 他的性格素直，从不拐弯抹角。
cn_example_pinyin: Tā de xìnggé sùzhí, cóng bù guǎiwān mò jiǎo.
cn_example_meaning: His personality is straightforward; he never beats around the bush.
meaning_shift_note: JP 素直 is an extremely common word meaning obedient/honest/docile with positive connotations. CN 素直 is not a standard modern compound — it may appear in literary contexts meaning 'plain and straightforward' but is rarely used. CN equivalents would be 老实 (lǎoshi), 坦率 (tǎnshuài), or 听话 (tīnghuà).
freq_note: Not a standard compound in modern Chinese — rarely encountered outside literary texts
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — not a standard modern Chinese compound

jp_example: 祖父は頑固で自分の意見を変えない。
jp_example_reading: そふはがんこでじぶんのいけんをかえない。
jp_example_meaning: My grandfather is stubborn and won't change his opinion.
cn_example: 他顽固地拒绝接受新思想。
cn_example_pinyin: Tā wángù de jùjué jiēshòu xīn sīxiǎng.
cn_example_meaning: He stubbornly refused to accept new ideas.
freq_note: Common in daily speech and literature — often with negative connotation
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — but widely used in daily Chinese

jp_example: 上司の横暴な態度に部下たちは不満を抱いている。
jp_example_reading: じょうしのおうぼうなたいどにぶかたちはふまんをいだいている。
jp_example_meaning: The subordinates are frustrated with the boss's tyrannical attitude.
cn_example: 侵略者的横暴行径激起了人民的反抗。
cn_example_pinyin: Qīnlüè zhě de héngbào xíngjìng jīqǐle rénmín de fǎnkàng.
cn_example_meaning: The invaders' brutal behavior aroused the people's resistance.
freq_note: Somewhat literary — 蛮横 (mánhèng) or 暴虐 (bàonüè) are more common in modern CN
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — literary/formal register

jp_example: 彼は貪欲に知識を吸収した。
jp_example_reading: かれはどんよくにちしきをきゅうしゅうした。
jp_example_meaning: He greedily absorbed knowledge.
cn_example: 贪欲是人类最大的弱点之一。
cn_example_pinyin: Tānyù shì rénlèi zuìdà de ruòdiǎn zhī yī.
cn_example_meaning: Greed is one of humanity's greatest weaknesses.
freq_note: Common in literature, news, and moral discussions — 贪婪 is also very common
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — literary/formal; related word 贪婪 (tānlán) is more colloquial

jp_example: 無謀な挑戦は危険だ。
jp_example_reading: むぼうなちょうせんはきけんだ。
jp_example_meaning: A reckless challenge is dangerous.
cn_example: 有勇无谋只会导致失败。
cn_example_pinyin: Yǒu yǒng wú móu zhǐ huì dǎozhì shībài.
cn_example_meaning: Having courage without strategy will only lead to failure.
meaning_shift_note: JP 無謀 means reckless/rash as a standalone adjective. CN 无谋 literally means 'without strategy' and mainly appears in the set phrase 有勇无谋 — it is not used as an independent adjective like in JP.
freq_note: Mainly appears in the idiom 有勇无谋 — not commonly used independently
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — mainly used in the classical phrase 有勇无谋

jp_example: 彼は冷酷な判断を下した。
jp_example_reading: かれはれいこくなはんだんをくだした。
jp_example_meaning: He made a cold-hearted decision.
cn_example: 现实是冷酷的，你必须面对。
cn_example_pinyin: Xiànshí shì lěngkù de, nǐ bìxū miànduì.
cn_example_meaning: Reality is cruel; you must face it.
freq_note: Very common — used in daily speech, literature, and media
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — but widely used in daily Chinese

jp_example: 彼は狡猾な手段で利益を得た。
jp_example_reading: かれはこうかつなしゅだんでりえきをえた。
jp_example_meaning: He gained profit through cunning means.
cn_example_pinyin: Nàge shāngrén fēicháng jiǎohuá.
cn_example_meaning: That businessman is very cunning.
freq_note: Common in daily speech and literature — consistently negative connotation

jp_example: 彼女は善良な人柄で皆に慕われている。
jp_example_reading: かのじょはぜんりょうなひとがらでみなにしたわれている。
jp_example_meaning: She is loved by everyone for her good-natured personality.
cn_example_pinyin: Tā shì yī gè shànliáng de rén.
cn_example_meaning: She is a kind-hearted person.
freq_note: Very common — used across all registers in daily life

jp_example: 彼女の気持ちは純粋だった。
jp_example_reading: かのじょのきもちはじゅんすいだった。
jp_example_meaning: Her feelings were pure.
cn_example_pinyin: Zhè chúncuì shì làngfèi shíjiān.
cn_example_meaning: This is purely a waste of time.
meaning_shift_note: JP 純粋 typically describes purity of character, emotions, or substances. CN 纯粹 is often used as an adverb meaning 'purely/simply/nothing but' in addition to the adjective sense of 'pure'.
freq_note: Very common — used as both adjective and adverb in daily Chinese

jp_example: 高速道路が渋滞している。
jp_example_reading: こうそくどうろがじゅうたいしている。
jp_example_meaning: The highway is congested.
cn_example_pinyin: Jīngjì fāzhǎn chūxiàn le tíngzhì.
cn_example_meaning: Economic development has stagnated.
meaning_shift_note: JP 渋滞 most commonly refers to traffic jams. The CN equivalent 停滞 refers to general stagnation and is not used for traffic. Traffic jam in CN is 堵车.
freq_note: Common in formal/written contexts about economics or progress
hsk_note: Not included in HSK 1-6

jp_example: これは汎用性の高いソフトウェアだ。
jp_example_reading: これははんようせいのたかいそふとうぇあだ。
jp_example_meaning: This is highly versatile software.
cn_example: 这个方法在很多领域通用。
cn_example_pinyin: Zhège fāngfǎ zài hěn duō lǐngyù tōngyòng.
cn_example_meaning: This method is applicable across many fields.
freq_note: Common in technical and everyday contexts
hsk_note: Not included in HSK 1-6

jp_example: 長年の経験が蓄積されている。
jp_example_reading: ながねんのけいけんがちくせきされている。
jp_example_meaning: Years of experience have been accumulated.
cn_example: 他积累了丰富的工作经验。
cn_example_pinyin: Tā jīlěi le fēngfù de gōngzuò jīngyàn.
cn_example_meaning: He has accumulated rich work experience.
freq_note: Very common in daily, academic, and business contexts

jp_example: 経費を削減する方針が決まった。
jp_example_reading: けいひをさくげんするほうしんがきまった。
jp_example_meaning: A policy to cut expenses has been decided.
cn_meaning: To cut; to reduce; to slash
cn_example: 政府决定削减财政支出。
cn_example_pinyin: Zhèngfǔ juédìng xuējiǎn cáizhèng zhīchū.
cn_example_meaning: The government decided to cut fiscal spending.
freq_note: Common in news and formal writing about budgets and policy
hsk_note: Not included in HSK 1-6

jp_example: 被害者に対して補償が行われた。
jp_example_reading: ひがいしゃにたいしてほしょうがおこなわれた。
jp_example_meaning: Compensation was provided to the victims.
cn_meaning: Compensation; to compensate; to make up for
cn_example_pinyin: Gōngsī huì bǔcháng nǐ de sǔnshī.
cn_example_meaning: The company will compensate your losses.
freq_note: Common in legal, business, and insurance contexts
hsk_note: Not included in HSK 1-6

jp_example: 剰余金を次年度に繰り越す。
jp_example_reading: じょうよきんをじねんどにくりこす。
jp_example_meaning: Carry over the surplus to the next fiscal year.
cn_example: 剩余的资金可以用于投资。
cn_example_pinyin: Shèngyú de zījīn kěyǐ yòngyú tóuzī.
cn_example_meaning: The surplus funds can be used for investment.
freq_note: Common in financial and economic contexts
hsk_note: Not included in HSK 1-6

jp_example: 水資源が枯渇しつつある。
jp_example_reading: みずしげんがこかつしつつある。
jp_example_meaning: Water resources are becoming depleted.
cn_example_pinyin: Shíyóu zīyuán zhèngzài kūjié.
cn_example_meaning: Oil resources are being depleted.
freq_note: Used in formal or written contexts about resources and energy
hsk_note: Not included in HSK 1-6

jp_example: 法律の効力が遡及的に適用される。
jp_example_reading: ほうりつのこうりょくがそきゅうてきにてきようされる。
jp_example_meaning: The law is applied retroactively.
cn_example_pinyin: Gāi guīdìng bù jùyǒu sùjí lì.
cn_example_meaning: This regulation does not have retroactive effect.
freq_note: Mostly used in legal and formal academic writing
hsk_note: Not included in HSK 1-6

jp_example: 競争に負けた企業は淘汰される。
jp_example_reading: きょうそうにまけたきぎょうはとうたされる。
jp_example_meaning: Companies that lose in competition are weeded out.
cn_meaning: To eliminate; to weed out; natural selection
cn_example: 落后的技术会被市场淘汰。
cn_example_pinyin: Luòhòu de jìshù huì bèi shìchǎng táotài.
cn_example_meaning: Outdated technology will be eliminated by the market.
freq_note: Very common in everyday speech, business, and news

jp_meaning: Following; inheriting a practice or precedent
jp_example: 前任者の方針を踏襲する。
jp_example_reading: ぜんにんしゃのほうしんをとうしゅうする。
jp_example_meaning: Follow the predecessor's policy.
cn_meaning: To follow (a tradition or convention); to carry on
cn_example_pinyin: Zhège xísú yánxí zhìjīn.
cn_example_meaning: This custom has been carried on to this day.
freq_note: Used in formal and written contexts about traditions and policies
hsk_note: Not included in HSK 1-6

jp_example: 規則から逸脱した行為は許されない。
jp_example_reading: きそくからいつだつしたこういはゆるされない。
jp_example_meaning: Actions that deviate from the rules are not permitted.
cn_meaning: To break away from; to separate from; to deviate
cn_example: 不能脱离实际来讨论问题。
cn_example_pinyin: Bù néng tuōlí shíjì lái tǎolùn wèntí.
cn_example_meaning: You can't discuss problems divorced from reality.
meaning_shift_note: JP 逸脱 emphasizes deviation from rules or norms. CN 脱离 emphasizes separation or breaking away from a group or reality, broader in usage.
freq_note: Very common in both spoken and written Chinese

jp_example: 新曲を披露する場が設けられた。
jp_example_reading: しんきょくをひろうするばがもうけられた。
jp_example_meaning: A venue was set up to unveil the new song.
cn_meaning: To disclose; to reveal; to announce publicly
cn_example: 公司披露了最新的财务报告。
cn_example_pinyin: Gōngsī pīlù le zuìxīn de cáiwù bàogào.
cn_example_meaning: The company disclosed its latest financial report.
meaning_shift_note: JP 披露 often refers to a public performance or presentation (e.g.,披露宴 = wedding reception). CN 披露 focuses on disclosure of information, especially in journalism and finance.
freq_note: Common in news and financial reporting
hsk_note: Not included in HSK 1-6

jp_meaning: Advocacy; proposal; to champion an idea
jp_example: 新しい理論を提唱した学者がいる。
jp_example_reading: あたらしいりろんをていしょうしたがくしゃがいる。
jp_example_meaning: There is a scholar who proposed a new theory.
cn_meaning: To advocate; to promote; to encourage
cn_example_pinyin: Zhèngfǔ tíchàng jiéyuē néngyuán.
cn_example_meaning: The government advocates energy conservation.
freq_note: Very common in news, policy discussions, and daily speech

jp_example: 任務を遂行するために全力を尽くす。
jp_example_reading: にんむをすいこうするためにぜんりょくをつくす。
jp_example_meaning: Give one's all to carry out the mission.
cn_meaning: To execute; to carry out; to implement
cn_example: 我们必须严格执行规定。
cn_example_pinyin: Wǒmen bìxū yángé zhíxíng guīdìng.
cn_example_meaning: We must strictly implement the regulations.
freq_note: Extremely common in all contexts — daily, business, legal

jp_example: 法律を遵守することは市民の義務である。
jp_example_reading: ほうりつをじゅんしゅすることはしみんのぎむである。
jp_example_meaning: Observing the law is a citizen's duty.
cn_meaning: To abide by; to observe; to comply with
cn_example: 每个人都应该遵守交通规则。
cn_example_pinyin: Měi gè rén dōu yīnggāi zūnshǒu jiāotōng guīzé.
cn_example_meaning: Everyone should observe traffic rules.
freq_note: Very common in legal, educational, and daily contexts

jp_example: 不正を是正する必要がある。
jp_example_reading: ふせいをぜせいするひつようがある。
jp_example_meaning: It is necessary to rectify the wrongdoing.
cn_meaning: To correct; to rectify; to put right
cn_example: 老师纠正了学生的发音错误。
cn_example_pinyin: Lǎoshī jiūzhèng le xuéshēng de fāyīn cuòwù.
cn_example_meaning: The teacher corrected the student's pronunciation errors.
freq_note: Very common in education, daily speech, and formal contexts

jp_example: 登録を抹消する手続きを行った。
jp_example_reading: とうろくをまっしょうするてつづきをおこなった。
jp_example_meaning: The procedure to delete the registration was carried out.
cn_meaning: To erase; to wipe out; to obliterate
cn_example: 这段痛苦的记忆无法抹除。
cn_example_pinyin: Zhè duàn tòngkǔ de jìyì wúfǎ mǒchú.
cn_example_meaning: This painful memory cannot be erased.
freq_note: Less common; 抹去 or 消除 are more frequently used in daily Chinese
hsk_note: Not included in HSK 1-6

jp_meaning: Setback; frustration; stalling of a plan
jp_example: 計画が資金不足で頓挫した。
jp_example_reading: けいかくがしきんぶそくでとんざした。
jp_example_meaning: The plan stalled due to lack of funding.
cn_example: 他的演讲抑扬顿挫，非常动听。
cn_example_pinyin: Tā de yǎnjiǎng yìyáng dùncuò, fēicháng dòngtīng.
cn_example_meaning: His speech had a rhythmic cadence and was very pleasant to listen to.
meaning_shift_note: JP 頓挫 means a setback or stalling of a project/plan. CN 顿挫 is primarily used in the literary expression 抑扬顿挫 to describe rhythmic cadence in speech or writing.
freq_note: Mostly used in the set phrase 抑扬顿挫; otherwise uncommon
hsk_note: Not included in HSK 1-6

jp_example: 彼の能力は他の候補者を凌駕している。
jp_example_reading: かれののうりょくはほかのこうほしゃをりょうがしている。
jp_example_meaning: His abilities surpass those of the other candidates.
cn_meaning: To override; to place oneself above; to surpass
cn_example: 任何人都不能凌驾于法律之上。
cn_example_pinyin: Rènhé rén dōu bù néng língjià yú fǎlǜ zhī shàng.
cn_example_meaning: No one can place themselves above the law.
meaning_shift_note: JP 凌駕 means to surpass in ability or achievement. CN 凌驾 often carries a negative connotation of overriding authority or placing oneself above something inappropriately.
freq_note: Used in formal or written Chinese, often with a critical tone
hsk_note: Not included in HSK 1-6

jp_example: 新しい勢力が台頭してきた。
jp_example_reading: あたらしいせいりょくがたいとうしてきた。
jp_example_meaning: A new force has risen to prominence.
cn_meaning: To raise one's head; to look up; (of a trend) to emerge
cn_example: 不良风气又有抬头的趋势。
cn_example_pinyin: Bùliáng fēngqì yòu yǒu táitóu de qūshì.
cn_example_meaning: Unhealthy trends are showing signs of re-emerging.
meaning_shift_note: JP 台頭 is a formal term for the rise/emergence of a power or movement. CN 抬头 literally means to raise one's head; its figurative use for re-emergence of a (usually negative) trend is secondary.
freq_note: Common in both literal (raise head) and figurative (trend emerging) uses
hsk_note: Not included in HSK 1-6

jp_example: 景気が沈滞している。
jp_example_reading: けいきがちんたいしている。
jp_example_meaning: The economy is stagnant.
cn_example_pinyin: Huìyì de qìfēn biàn de chénzhì.
cn_example_meaning: The atmosphere of the meeting became stagnant.
freq_note: Relatively literary; 沉闷 or 低迷 are more commonly used in daily speech
hsk_note: Not included in HSK 1-6

jp_example: この商店街はとても繁盛している。
jp_example_reading: このしょうてんがいはとてもはんじょうしている。
jp_example_meaning: This shopping street is thriving.
cn_example: 这座城市的商业非常繁盛。
cn_example_pinyin: Zhè zuò chéngshì de shāngyè fēicháng fánshèng.
cn_example_meaning: The commerce of this city is very prosperous.
freq_note: Less common than 繁荣; used more in literary or classical contexts
hsk_note: Not included in HSK 1-6; 繁荣 is in HSK 6

jp_example: 文化の隆盛を遂げた時代であった。
jp_example_reading: ぶんかのりゅうせいをとげたじだいであった。
jp_example_meaning: It was an era that achieved cultural prosperity.
cn_example_pinyin: Tángcháo shíqī guólì lóngshèng.
cn_example_meaning: During the Tang Dynasty, national power was at its peak.
freq_note: Literary and historical; 繁荣 or 兴盛 are more common in modern usage
hsk_note: Not included in HSK 1-6

jp_example: 病気で体が衰弱してしまった。
jp_example_reading: びょうきでからだがすいじゃくしてしまった。
jp_example_meaning: The body became debilitated due to illness.
cn_example: 他因长期生病而身体衰弱。
cn_example_pinyin: Tā yīn chángqī shēngbìng ér shēntǐ shuāiruò.
cn_example_meaning: He is physically debilitated from long-term illness.
freq_note: Common in medical and literary contexts
hsk_note: Not included in HSK 1-6

jp_example: 長時間の労働で疲労が蓄積した。
jp_example_reading: ちょうじかんのろうどうでひろうがちくせきした。
jp_example_meaning: Fatigue accumulated from long hours of work.
cn_example: 开车时间太长容易产生疲劳。
cn_example_pinyin: Kāichē shíjiān tài cháng róngyì chǎnshēng píláo.
cn_example_meaning: Driving for too long can easily cause fatigue.
freq_note: Very common in daily life, health, and work contexts

jp_example: 事件の経緯を克明に記録した。
jp_example_reading: じけんのけいいをこくめいにきろくした。
jp_example_meaning: The course of events was recorded in meticulous detail.
cn_example_pinyin: Qǐng xiángxì shuōmíng yīxià qíngkuàng.
cn_example_meaning: Please explain the situation in detail.
freq_note: Extremely common in all contexts — daily, academic, business

jp_example: 綿密な計画を立てる必要がある。
jp_example_reading: めんみつなけいかくをたてるひつようがある。
jp_example_meaning: It is necessary to make a meticulous plan.
cn_example_pinyin: Tā de sīwéi fēicháng zhěnmì.
cn_example_meaning: His thinking is very meticulous.
freq_note: Somewhat literary; 仔细 or 周密 are more commonly used in daily speech
hsk_note: Not included in HSK 1-6

jp_example: 簡素な生活を心がけている。
jp_example_reading: かんそなせいかつをこころがけている。
jp_example_meaning: I try to live a simple life.
cn_example_pinyin: Tā guòzhe jiǎnpǔ de shēnghuó.
cn_example_meaning: She lives a simple and frugal life.
freq_note: Common in describing lifestyle, design, and character
hsk_note: Not included in HSK 1-6; 简单 and 朴素 are in HSK

jp_example: 雄大な自然の景色に圧倒された。
jp_example_reading: ゆうだいなしぜんのけしきにあっとうされた。
jp_example_meaning: I was overwhelmed by the grand natural scenery.
cn_example_pinyin: Chángchéng fēicháng xióngwěi zhuàngguān.
cn_example_meaning: The Great Wall is very grand and magnificent.
freq_note: Common when describing landscapes, buildings, and achievements
hsk_note: Not included in HSK 1-6

jp_example: 高地では空気が希薄になる。
jp_example_reading: こうちではくうきがきはくになる。
jp_example_meaning: The air becomes thin at high altitudes.
cn_example_pinyin: Gāoyuán shàng kōngqì xībó.
cn_example_meaning: The air is thin on the plateau.
freq_note: Common in scientific, geographic, and figurative contexts
hsk_note: Not included in HSK 1-6

jp_example: 犯罪の疑いが濃厚になった。
jp_example_reading: はんざいのうたがいがのうこうになった。
jp_example_meaning: Suspicion of a crime became strong.
cn_example: 这里的学术气氛非常浓厚。
cn_example_pinyin: Zhèlǐ de xuéshù qìfēn fēicháng nónghòu.
cn_example_meaning: The academic atmosphere here is very strong.
freq_note: Common in describing atmosphere, interest, flavor, and color
hsk_note: Not included in HSK 1-6

jp_example: 問題が顕在化してきた。
jp_example_reading: もんだいがけんざいかしてきた。
jp_example_meaning: The problem has become apparent.
cn_meaning: To appear; to become visible; to manifest
cn_example_pinyin: Wèntí zhújiàn xiǎnxiàn chūlái.
cn_example_meaning: The problem gradually became apparent.
freq_note: Common in formal and written contexts
hsk_note: Not included in HSK 1-6

jp_example: 台風による被害は甚大であった。
jp_example_reading: たいふうによるひがいはじんだいであった。
jp_example_meaning: The damage from the typhoon was enormous.
cn_example: 这个项目需要巨大的投资。
cn_example_pinyin: Zhège xiàngmù xūyào jùdà de tóuzī.
cn_example_meaning: This project requires enormous investment.
freq_note: Extremely common in all contexts

jp_example: 彼は著名な作家として知られている。
jp_example_reading: かれはちょめいなさっかとしてしられている。
jp_example_meaning: He is known as a famous writer.
cn_example: 他是一位著名的科学家。
cn_example_pinyin: Tā shì yī wèi zhùmíng de kēxuéjiā.
cn_example_meaning: He is a famous scientist.
freq_note: Very common in all contexts — news, daily speech, academic

jp_example: これは典型的な日本建築だ。
jp_example_reading: これはてんけいてきなにほんけんちくだ。
jp_example_meaning: This is a typical Japanese building.
cn_example_pinyin: Zhè shì yī gè diǎnxíng de lìzi.
cn_example_meaning: This is a typical example.
freq_note: Very common in all contexts — daily, academic, news

jp_example: 架空の人物を題材にした小説だ。
jp_example_reading: かくうのじんぶつをだいざいにしたしょうせつだ。
jp_example_meaning: It is a novel based on a fictional character.
cn_example: 架空电线需要定期检修。
cn_example_pinyin: Jiàkōng diànxiàn xūyào dìngqī jiǎnxiū.
cn_example_meaning: Overhead power lines need regular maintenance.
meaning_shift_note: JP 架空 primarily means fictional/imaginary. CN 架空 primarily means overhead/elevated (for wires/cables) or baseless, and is also used in internet slang for alternate history fiction.
freq_note: Used in technical contexts for overhead structures; also in online fiction communities
hsk_note: Not included in HSK 1-6

jp_example: 暫定的な措置として予算を組んだ。
jp_example_reading: ざんていてきなそちとしてよさんをくんだ。
jp_example_meaning: A budget was drawn up as a provisional measure.
cn_example: 会议时间暂定为下周三。
cn_example_pinyin: Huìyì shíjiān zàndìng wéi xià zhōu sān.
cn_example_meaning: The meeting time is tentatively set for next Wednesday.
freq_note: Common in business, scheduling, and official communications
hsk_note: Not included in HSK 1-6

jp_example: 恒久的な平和を目指す。
jp_example_reading: こうきゅうてきなへいわをめざす。
jp_example_meaning: Aim for permanent peace.
cn_example_pinyin: Rénmen kěwàng héngjiǔ de hépíng.
cn_example_meaning: People long for lasting peace.
freq_note: Somewhat literary; 永久 or 永恒 are more commonly used in modern Chinese
hsk_note: Not included in HSK 1-6

jp_example: 漸進的に改革を進めるべきだ。
jp_example_reading: ぜんしんてきにかいかくをすすめるべきだ。
jp_example_meaning: Reforms should be advanced gradually.
cn_example_pinyin: Xuéxí yǔyán yào xúnxù jiànjìn.
cn_example_meaning: Learning a language requires a step-by-step approach.
freq_note: Common in education, policy, and self-improvement contexts; often in 循序渐进
hsk_note: Not included in HSK 1-6 as standalone; 循序渐进 is a common idiom

jp_example: 斬新なアイデアで注目を集めた。
jp_example_reading: ざんしんなあいであでちゅうもくをあつめた。
jp_example_meaning: Attracted attention with a novel idea.
cn_example_pinyin: Zhège shèjì fēicháng xīnyǐng.
cn_example_meaning: This design is very novel.
freq_note: Common in design, technology, and creative contexts
hsk_note: Not included in HSK 1-6

jp_example: 抜本的な対策が求められている。
jp_example_reading: ばっぽんてきなたいさくがもとめられている。
jp_example_meaning: Fundamental measures are being called for.
cn_meaning: Fundamental; basic; root; at all (in negation)
cn_example: 我们需要从根本上解决问题。
cn_example_pinyin: Wǒmen xūyào cóng gēnběn shàng jiějué wèntí.
cn_example_meaning: We need to solve the problem fundamentally.
meaning_shift_note: JP 抜本 is primarily used as an adjective (抜本的) meaning drastic/radical reform. CN 根本 is broader — it means fundamental/basic and is also used as an adverb meaning 'at all' in negative sentences.
freq_note: Extremely common in all contexts as both adjective and adverb

jp_example: 粗雑な作りの家具は長持ちしない。
jp_example_reading: そざつなつくりのかぐはながもちしない。
jp_example_meaning: Crudely made furniture does not last long.
cn_example_pinyin: Zhè kuài mùbǎn biǎomiàn hěn cūcāo.
cn_example_meaning: The surface of this wooden board is very rough.
freq_note: Common in daily speech for describing texture, quality, and workmanship
hsk_note: Not included in HSK 1-6

jp_example: 緻密な分析に基づいた報告書だ。
jp_example_reading: ちみつなぶんせきにもとづいたほうこくしょだ。
jp_example_meaning: It is a report based on meticulous analysis.
cn_example: 这种材料结构致密，非常坚固。
cn_example_pinyin: Zhè zhǒng cáiliào jiégòu zhìmì, fēicháng jiāngù.
cn_example_meaning: This material has a dense structure and is very strong.
meaning_shift_note: JP 緻密 means meticulous/elaborate in terms of work or planning. CN 致密 primarily describes physical density/compactness of materials. For meticulous in CN, 精密 or 缜密 are used.
freq_note: Mostly used in scientific and technical contexts about material density
hsk_note: Not included in HSK 1-6

jp_example: 過剰な期待はプレッシャーになる。
jp_example_reading: かじょうなきたいはぷれっしゃーになる。
jp_example_meaning: Excessive expectations become pressure.
cn_example_pinyin: Mùqián shìchǎng shàng chǎnnéng guòshèng.
cn_example_meaning: There is currently overcapacity in the market.
freq_note: Common in economic and business contexts
hsk_note: Not included in HSK 1-6

jp_example: 稀少な動物を保護する活動が行われている。
jp_example_reading: きしょうなどうぶつをほごするかつどうがおこなわれている。
jp_example_meaning: Activities to protect rare animals are being conducted.
cn_example_pinyin: Zhè zhǒng kuàngwù fēicháng xīshǎo.
cn_example_meaning: This type of mineral is very rare.
freq_note: Common in science, nature, and collectibles contexts
hsk_note: Not included in HSK 1-6; 稀有 is a more common synonym

jp_example: 栄養の均衡が取れた食事が大切だ。
jp_example_reading: えいようのきんこうがとれたしょくじがたいせつだ。
jp_example_meaning: A nutritionally balanced diet is important.
cn_example: 经济发展要注意区域均衡。
cn_example_pinyin: Jīngjì fāzhǎn yào zhùyì qūyù jūnhéng.
cn_example_meaning: Economic development should pay attention to regional balance.
freq_note: Common in economics, health, and policy discussions
hsk_note: Not included in HSK 1-6

jp_example: この建物は左右対称のデザインだ。
jp_example_reading: このたてものはさゆうたいしょうのでざいんだ。
jp_example_meaning: This building has a symmetrical design.
cn_example: 人的脸并不是完全对称的。
cn_example_pinyin: Rén de liǎn bìng bù shì wánquán duìchèn de.
cn_example_meaning: A person's face is not perfectly symmetrical.
freq_note: Common in math, science, art, and design contexts
hsk_note: Not included in HSK 1-6

jp_example: 二つのプロジェクトを並行して進める。
jp_example_reading: ふたつのぷろじぇくとをへいこうしてすすめる。
jp_example_meaning: Advance two projects in parallel.
cn_meaning: To run in parallel; concurrent; simultaneous
cn_example: 这两个方案可以并行实施。
cn_example_pinyin: Zhè liǎng gè fāng'àn kěyǐ bìngxíng shíshī.
cn_example_meaning: These two plans can be implemented in parallel.
freq_note: Common in business, technology, and policy contexts
hsk_note: Not included in HSK 1-6

jp_example: 両者の間に第三者が介在している。
jp_example_reading: りょうしゃのあいだにだいさんしゃがかいざいしている。
jp_example_meaning: A third party is interposed between the two.
cn_meaning: To intervene; to get involved in
cn_example: 外国不应该介入他国内政。
cn_example_pinyin: Wàiguó bù yīnggāi jièrù tā guó nèizhèng.
cn_example_meaning: Foreign countries should not intervene in other nations' internal affairs.
meaning_shift_note: JP 介在 means something exists or is interposed between two things (neutral). CN 介入 means to actively intervene or get involved (more active/intentional).
freq_note: Very common in news, politics, and daily speech
hsk_note: Not included in HSK 1-6

jp_example: 連鎖反応が次々と起こった。
jp_example_reading: れんさはんのうがつぎつぎとおこった。
jp_example_meaning: Chain reactions occurred one after another.
cn_example: 这是一家全国连锁的超市。
cn_example_pinyin: Zhè shì yī jiā quánguó liánsuǒ de chāoshì.
cn_example_meaning: This is a nationwide chain supermarket.
meaning_shift_note: JP 連鎖 emphasizes chain reactions and linked sequences of events. CN 连锁 is most commonly used for chain stores/businesses (连锁店), though 连锁反应 (chain reaction) also exists.
freq_note: Very common — 连锁店 (chain store) is used daily
hsk_note: Not included in HSK 1-6

jp_example: 大雨で家が浸水した。
jp_example_reading: おおあめでいえがしんすいした。
jp_example_meaning: The house was flooded due to heavy rain.
cn_meaning: To be submerged in water; to soak in water
cn_example_pinyin: Dìxiàshì bèi jìn shuǐ le.
cn_example_meaning: The basement was flooded.
freq_note: Used in news and disaster reports; not common in daily speech
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — technical/disaster-related vocabulary

jp_example: 冬は空気が乾燥している。
jp_example_reading: ふゆはくうきがかんそうしている。
jp_example_meaning: The air is dry in winter.
cn_example_pinyin: Zhèlǐ de qìhòu fēicháng gān zào.
cn_example_meaning: The climate here is very dry.
freq_note: Very common — used for weather, skin care, and figuratively for boring topics

jp_example: 記憶が風化しないように記録を残す。
jp_example_reading: きおくがふうかしないようにきろくをのこす。
jp_example_meaning: We keep records so that memories don't fade away.
cn_example: 岩石经过长年的风化变得很脆弱。
cn_example_pinyin: Yánshí jīngguò chángnián de fēnghuà biàn de hěn cuìruò.
cn_example_meaning: The rock became fragile after years of weathering.
meaning_shift_note: JP uses 風化 both for geological weathering and figuratively for memories fading. CN uses it for geological weathering and also for public morals (伤风败俗), a meaning absent in JP.
freq_note: Used in geology and formal/literary contexts; not everyday vocabulary
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — specialized geological or literary term

jp_example: 災害に備えて非常食を準備する。
jp_example_reading: さいがいにそなえてひじょうしょくをじゅんびする。
jp_example_meaning: Prepare emergency food in case of a disaster.
cn_example: 政府积极应对自然灾害。
cn_example_pinyin: Zhèngfǔ jījí yìngduì zìrán zāihài.
cn_example_meaning: The government actively responds to natural disasters.
freq_note: Very common in news, government reports, and daily conversation

jp_example: 河川の汚濁が深刻な問題になっている。
jp_example_reading: かせんのおだくがしんこくなもんだいになっている。
jp_example_meaning: River pollution has become a serious problem.
cn_meaning: Dirty; turbid; foul (of air or water)
cn_example_pinyin: Zhè tiáo hé de shuǐ hěn wūzhuó.
cn_example_meaning: The water in this river is very murky.
freq_note: Used in written/formal contexts; spoken Chinese prefers 脏 or 浑浊
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — formal/literary vocabulary

jp_example: 生態系のバランスを守ることが大切だ。
jp_example_reading: せいたいけいのバランスをまもることがたいせつだ。
jp_example_meaning: It is important to protect the balance of the ecosystem.
cn_example_pinyin: Wǒmen yào bǎohù shēngtài huánjìng.
cn_example_meaning: We need to protect the ecological environment.
freq_note: Very common in news, policy discussions, and environmental topics

jp_meaning: Inhabiting; living (of animals/organisms in a habitat)
jp_example: この森には珍しい動物が生息している。
jp_example_reading: このもりにはめずらしいどうぶつがせいそくしている。
jp_example_meaning: Rare animals inhabit this forest.
cn_meaning: To grow and multiply; interest (on money); livelihood
cn_example: 这种鸟类在湿地生息繁衍。
cn_example_pinyin: Zhè zhǒng niǎolèi zài shīdì shēngxī fányǎn.
cn_example_meaning: This kind of bird lives and breeds in wetlands.
meaning_shift_note: JP 生息 specifically means animals/organisms living in a habitat. CN 生息 has broader meanings including growing/multiplying, earning interest on money, and livelihood.
freq_note: Less common in modern CN; 栖息 (qīxī) is more commonly used for animal habitation
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — literary/formal term; 栖息 is more standard

jp_example: 多くの生物が絶滅の危機に瀕している。
jp_example_reading: おおくのせいぶつがぜつめつのききにひんしている。
jp_example_meaning: Many species are on the verge of extinction.
cn_example: 恐龙在六千五百万年前绝灭了。
cn_example_pinyin: Kǒnglóng zài liùqiān wǔbǎi wàn nián qián juémiè le.
cn_example_meaning: Dinosaurs went extinct 65 million years ago.
freq_note: Used in formal/scientific writing; spoken CN more commonly uses 灭绝 (mièjué)
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — 灭绝 is the more common CN variant

jp_example: 逃げた動物が無事に捕獲された。
jp_example_reading: にげたどうぶつがぶじにほかくされた。
jp_example_meaning: The escaped animal was safely captured.
cn_meaning: To capture; to catch; to seize
cn_example_pinyin: Jǐngchá bǔhuò le táofàn.
cn_example_meaning: The police captured the fugitive.
freq_note: Common in news reports about crime and wildlife
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — formal/news vocabulary; related 捕 appears in simpler words

jp_example: この地域では米の栽培が盛んだ。
jp_example_reading: このちいきではこめのさいばいがさかんだ。
jp_example_meaning: Rice cultivation is thriving in this area.
cn_meaning: To cultivate; to grow (plants); to nurture (talent)
cn_example: 农民栽培了大量的蔬菜。
cn_example_pinyin: Nóngmín zāipéi le dàliàng de shūcài.
cn_example_meaning: The farmers grew a large quantity of vegetables.
freq_note: Common in agriculture and gardening contexts; also used figuratively for nurturing talent

jp_example: 養殖のサーモンが市場に出回っている。
jp_example_reading: ようしょくのサーモンがしじょうにでまわっている。
jp_example_meaning: Farm-raised salmon is available on the market.
cn_meaning: To breed; to raise; aquaculture; farming (animals)
cn_example: 这个地区以养殖淡水鱼闻名。
cn_example_pinyin: Zhège dìqū yǐ yǎngzhí dànshuǐ yú wénmíng.
cn_example_meaning: This area is famous for freshwater fish farming.
freq_note: Common in agricultural and food industry discussions
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — industry-specific term

jp_example: 納豆は大豆を発酵させた食品だ。
jp_example_reading: なっとうはだいずをはっこうさせたしょくひんだ。
jp_example_meaning: Natto is a food made by fermenting soybeans.
cn_example: 面团需要发酵两个小时。
cn_example_pinyin: Miàntuán xūyào fājiào liǎng gè xiǎoshí.
cn_example_meaning: The dough needs to ferment for two hours.
freq_note: Common in food, cooking, and biology contexts; also used figuratively for escalating situations
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — food science and biology term

jp_example: 汚染された水を浄化する技術が開発された。
jp_example_reading: おせんされたみずをじょうかするぎじゅつがかいはつされた。
jp_example_meaning: Technology to purify contaminated water was developed.
cn_example: 这个设备可以净化空气。
cn_example_pinyin: Zhège shèbèi kěyǐ jìnghuà kōngqì.
cn_example_meaning: This device can purify the air.
freq_note: Common in environmental, health, and spiritual contexts
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — technical/environmental vocabulary

jp_example: 建物の劣化が進んでいる。
jp_example_reading: たてもののれっかがすすんでいる。
jp_example_meaning: The deterioration of the building is progressing.
cn_example: 材料劣化会影响产品的安全性。
cn_example_pinyin: Cáiliào lièhuà huì yǐngxiǎng chǎnpǐn de ānquánxìng.
cn_example_meaning: Material degradation affects product safety.
freq_note: Used in technical and engineering contexts; not common in daily speech
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — technical/engineering vocabulary

jp_example: 都市の変遷を歴史的に振り返る。
jp_example_reading: としのへんせんをれきしてきにふりかえる。
jp_example_meaning: Looking back historically at the transitions of the city.
cn_example: 社会经历了巨大的变迁。
cn_example_pinyin: Shèhuì jīnglì le jùdà de biànqiān.
cn_example_meaning: Society has undergone tremendous changes.
freq_note: Common in historical, academic, and news contexts
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — formal/literary vocabulary

jp_example: 伝統文化を次世代に継承する。
jp_example_reading: でんとうぶんかをじせだいにけいしょうする。
jp_example_meaning: Pass on traditional culture to the next generation.
cn_meaning: To inherit; to succeed to; inheritance
cn_example_pinyin: Tā jìchéng le fùqīn de shìyè.
cn_example_meaning: He inherited his father's business.
freq_note: Very common — used for inheritance of property, traditions, and legacy

jp_example: この祭りは古くから伝承されてきた。
jp_example_reading: このまつりはふるくからでんしょうされてきた。
jp_example_meaning: This festival has been handed down since ancient times.
cn_meaning: To pass down; to inherit and carry on (culture, skills)
cn_example: 非物质文化遗产需要传承。
cn_example_pinyin: Fēi wùzhì wénhuà yíchǎn xūyào chuánchéng.
cn_example_meaning: Intangible cultural heritage needs to be passed down.
freq_note: Common in cultural, historical, and policy discussions
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — cultural/formal vocabulary

jp_example: 生命の起源についてはまだ多くの謎がある。
jp_example_reading: せいめいのきげんについてはまだおおくのなぞがある。
jp_example_meaning: There are still many mysteries about the origin of life.
cn_example_pinyin: Rénlèi wénmíng qǐyuán yú héchù?
cn_example_meaning: Where did human civilization originate?
freq_note: Common in academic, scientific, and historical discussions
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — academic vocabulary

jp_example: この地名の由来を調べた。
jp_example_reading: このちめいのゆらいをしらべた。
jp_example_meaning: I looked into the origin of this place name.
cn_example_pinyin: Zhège xísú yóulái yǐ jiǔ.
cn_example_meaning: This custom has a long history.
meaning_shift_note: JP 由来 is primarily a noun meaning origin/derivation. CN 由来 can mean origin but is more often used adverbially to mean 'since/all along' in classical or formal expressions.
freq_note: Used in formal/literary Chinese; less common in everyday speech
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — literary/classical expression

jp_example: この問題から派生した課題が多い。
jp_example_reading: このもんだいからはせいしたかだいがおおい。
jp_example_meaning: Many issues have derived from this problem.
cn_example: 这个词派生出了很多新词。
cn_example_pinyin: Zhège cí pàishēng chū le hěn duō xīn cí.
cn_example_meaning: This word has spawned many new words.
freq_note: Used in academic and technical contexts; not common in daily conversation
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — academic/technical vocabulary

jp_example: その影響は他の業界にも波及した。
jp_example_reading: そのえいきょうはほかのぎょうかいにもはきゅうした。
jp_example_meaning: The impact spread to other industries as well.
cn_meaning: To spread to; to affect; to ripple out
cn_example_pinyin: Jīngjì wēijī bōjí quánqiú.
cn_example_meaning: The economic crisis spread across the globe.
freq_note: Common in news and formal discussions about economics and policy
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — formal/news vocabulary

jp_example: 設備の拡充を計画している。
jp_example_reading: せつびのかくじゅうをけいかくしている。
jp_example_meaning: We are planning to expand the facilities.
cn_meaning: To expand; to enlarge; to augment
cn_example: 公司计划扩充团队规模。
cn_example_pinyin: Gōngsī jìhuà kuòchōng tuánduì guīmó.
cn_example_meaning: The company plans to expand the team size.
freq_note: Common in business and organizational contexts
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — business/formal vocabulary; related 扩大 is HSK 5

jp_example: 充実した生活を送りたい。
jp_example_reading: じゅうじつしたせいかつをおくりたい。
jp_example_meaning: I want to lead a fulfilling life.
cn_meaning: Substantial; rich; to enrich; to replenish
cn_example: 我们要不断充实自己的知识。
cn_example_pinyin: Wǒmen yào bùduàn chōngshí zìjǐ de zhīshì.
cn_example_meaning: We should constantly enrich our knowledge.
freq_note: Very common — used in daily life, education, and self-improvement contexts

jp_example: 飽食の時代と言われている。
jp_example_reading: ほうしょくのじだいといわれている。
jp_example_meaning: It is said to be an age of overindulgence.
cn_meaning: To eat one's fill; to eat to satiety
cn_example: 饱食终日无所事事是不好的。
cn_example_pinyin: Bǎoshí zhōngrì wú suǒ shìshì shì bù hǎo de.
cn_example_meaning: It's not good to eat one's fill all day and do nothing.
meaning_shift_note: JP 飽食 emphasizes excess and overindulgence (negative connotation about modern society). CN 饱食 simply means eating to fullness, often appearing in the idiom 饱食终日 (idle after eating).
freq_note: Used mainly in classical expressions like 饱食终日; not common in modern daily speech
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — literary/classical vocabulary

jp_example: 健康のために食事を節制する。
jp_example_reading: けんこうのためにしょくじをせっせいする。
jp_example_meaning: Exercise moderation in eating for the sake of health.
cn_meaning: To control; to moderate; moderation; temperance
cn_example_pinyin: Yǐnshí yào yǒu jiézhì.
cn_example_meaning: One should exercise moderation in eating and drinking.
freq_note: Used in health and lifestyle discussions; somewhat formal
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — formal/literary vocabulary

jp_example: 禁欲的な生活を送る修行僧もいる。
jp_example_reading: きんよくてきなせいかつをおくるしゅぎょうそうもいる。
jp_example_meaning: There are monks who lead an ascetic life.
cn_example_pinyin: Tā guò zhe jìnyù de shēnghuó.
cn_example_meaning: He lives an ascetic life.
freq_note: Used in religious, philosophical, and lifestyle discussions
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — philosophical/religious vocabulary

jp_example: たまには贅沢な食事を楽しみたい。
jp_example_reading: たまにはぜいたくなしょくじをたのしみたい。
jp_example_meaning: I want to enjoy a luxurious meal once in a while.
cn_example_pinyin: Bùyào guò shēchǐ de shēnghuó.
cn_example_meaning: Don't live an extravagant life.
freq_note: Common in discussions about lifestyle, economics, and consumption
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — common in spoken and written Chinese but not in HSK syllabus

jp_example: 質素な暮らしを心がけている。
jp_example_reading: しっそなくらしをこころがけている。
jp_example_meaning: I try to lead a simple lifestyle.
cn_meaning: Quality; nature; character (of a person or thing)
cn_example_pinyin: Tā de zhìsù hěn gāo.
cn_example_meaning: She is of high quality/caliber.
meaning_shift_note: JP 質素 means simple/frugal lifestyle. CN 质素 means quality/character/caliber of a person — completely different meaning. The CN equivalent of JP 質素 would be 朴素 (pǔsù).
freq_note: Used mainly in Cantonese-influenced Mandarin and formal writing; not standard in mainland daily speech
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — more common in Cantonese Chinese

jp_example: 素朴な疑問を持つことが大切だ。
jp_example_reading: そぼくなぎもんをもつことがたいせつだ。
jp_example_meaning: It is important to have simple, honest questions.
cn_example: 她穿着素朴，但很有气质。
cn_example_pinyin: Tā chuān zhe sùpǔ, dàn hěn yǒu qìzhì.
cn_example_meaning: She dresses simply, but has great elegance.
freq_note: Used in literary/formal contexts; spoken CN more commonly uses 朴素 (pǔsù)
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — literary variant; 朴素 is more standard

jp_example: 雑多な情報の中から必要なものを選ぶ。
jp_example_reading: ざったなじょうほうのなかからひつようなものをえらぶ。
jp_example_meaning: Select what you need from miscellaneous information.
cn_meaning: Miscellaneous and numerous; diverse and varied
cn_example: 市场上的商品杂多，令人眼花缭乱。
cn_example_pinyin: Shìchǎng shàng de shāngpǐn záduō, lìng rén yǎnhuā liáoluàn.
cn_example_meaning: The goods on the market are diverse and dazzling.
freq_note: Used in formal/written contexts; not common in daily speech
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — formal/literary vocabulary

jp_example: 煩雑な手続きを簡略化する必要がある。
jp_example_reading: はんざつなてつづきをかんりゃくかするひつようがある。
jp_example_meaning: There is a need to simplify cumbersome procedures.
cn_meaning: Complicated and miscellaneous; tedious and disordered
cn_example: 日常事务繁杂，让人疲惫不堪。
cn_example_pinyin: Rìcháng shìwù fánzá, ràng rén píbèi bùkān.
cn_example_meaning: Daily affairs are tedious and exhausting.
freq_note: Used in formal/written contexts; spoken CN often uses 麻烦 or 复杂 instead
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — formal vocabulary; 繁杂 (fánzá) is the more common CN variant

jp_example: 議論は多岐にわたった。
jp_example_reading: ぎろんはたきにわたった。
jp_example_meaning: The discussion covered a wide range of topics.
cn_example: 多歧亡羊，学者们各执一词。
cn_example_pinyin: Duō qí wáng yáng, xuézhěmen gè zhí yī cí.
cn_example_meaning: Too many paths lose the sheep — scholars each hold their own opinions.
meaning_shift_note: JP 多岐 is commonly used as an adjective meaning varied/diverse (多岐にわたる). CN 多歧 is rare and mainly appears in the classical idiom 多歧亡羊, meaning confusion from too many choices.
freq_note: Rare in modern CN; mainly found in the classical allusion 多歧亡羊
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — classical Chinese allusion

jp_example: 全員一律に同じ条件が適用される。
jp_example_reading: ぜんいんいちりつにおなじじょうけんがてきようされる。
jp_example_meaning: The same conditions apply uniformly to everyone.
cn_example_pinyin: Chídào zhě yīlǜ kòu fēn.
cn_example_meaning: Latecomers will all have points deducted without exception.
freq_note: Common in rules, regulations, and formal announcements

jp_example: 画一的な教育では個性が育たない。
jp_example_reading: かくいつてきなきょういくではこせいがそだたない。
jp_example_meaning: Standardized education does not nurture individuality.
cn_example: 公司要求统一划一的着装标准。
cn_example_pinyin: Gōngsī yāoqiú tǒngyī huàyī de zhuózhuāng biāozhǔn.
cn_example_meaning: The company requires a uniform dress code standard.
freq_note: Used in formal/administrative contexts; not common in daily speech
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — formal/administrative vocabulary

jp_example: 多様な価値観を尊重することが大切だ。
jp_example_reading: たようなかちかんをそんちょうすることがたいせつだ。
jp_example_meaning: It is important to respect diverse values.
cn_example: 生物多样性对生态系统至关重要。
cn_example_pinyin: Shēngwù duōyàngxìng duì shēngtài xìtǒng zhìguān zhòngyào.
cn_example_meaning: Biodiversity is crucial to the ecosystem.
freq_note: Very common — used in science, policy, daily conversation, and media

jp_example: 柔軟な発想が求められている。
jp_example_reading: じゅうなんなはっそうがもとめられている。
jp_example_meaning: Flexible thinking is being demanded.
cn_example_pinyin: Zhè kuài bùliào shǒugǎn hěn róuruǎn.
cn_example_meaning: This fabric feels very soft.
meaning_shift_note: JP 柔軟 emphasizes mental/strategic flexibility and adaptability. CN 柔软 primarily describes physical softness/suppleness of materials, though it can occasionally be used figuratively.
freq_note: Common for describing textures, fabrics, and physical softness

jp_example: この建物は頑丈に造られている。
jp_example_reading: このたてものはがんじょうにつくられている。
jp_example_meaning: This building is built sturdily.
cn_example_pinyin: Zhè zuò chéngqiáng fēicháng jiāngù.
cn_example_meaning: This city wall is very sturdy.
freq_note: Common in descriptions of buildings, structures, and objects
