hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — descriptive vocabulary; related 坚 appears in HSK words

jp_example: 堅固な要塞を築いた。
jp_example_reading: けんごなようさいをきずいた。
jp_example_meaning: They built an impregnable fortress.
cn_example: 这座桥的结构非常坚固。
cn_example_pinyin: Zhè zuò qiáo de jiégòu fēicháng jiāngù.
cn_example_meaning: The structure of this bridge is very solid.
freq_note: Common in descriptions of structures, materials, and figuratively for resolve
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — descriptive/formal vocabulary

jp_example: 証拠が薄弱で立証できなかった。
jp_example_reading: しょうこがはくじゃくでりっしょうできなかった。
jp_example_meaning: The evidence was too weak to prove the case.
cn_meaning: Weak; frail; thin (of foundation, willpower, etc.)
cn_example_pinyin: Tā de yìzhìlì bǐjiào bóruò.
cn_example_meaning: His willpower is rather weak.
freq_note: Common in formal and academic contexts for describing weakness of foundations, arguments, or willpower
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — formal/written vocabulary

jp_example: 強靭な精神力で困難を乗り越えた。
jp_example_reading: きょうじんなせいしんりょくでこんなんをのりこえた。
jp_example_meaning: He overcame difficulties with tough mental strength.
cn_meaning: Tough; tenacious; resilient; strong and flexible
cn_example_pinyin: Zhúzi jùyǒu qiángrèn de tèxìng.
cn_example_meaning: Bamboo has tough and resilient properties.
freq_note: Used in formal and literary contexts; spoken CN often uses 坚韧 (jiānrèn)
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — literary vocabulary; 坚韧 is more commonly used

jp_example: 彼は好奇心が旺盛だ。
jp_example_reading: かれはこうきしんがおうせいだ。
jp_example_meaning: He has a vigorous curiosity.
cn_example_pinyin: Niánqīngrén jīnglì wàngshèng.
cn_example_meaning: Young people are full of energy.
freq_note: Common in descriptions of energy, vitality, and demand
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — descriptive vocabulary commonly encountered in media

jp_example: 彼女は危機的状況でも沈着に対応した。
jp_example_reading: かのじょはききてきじょうきょうでもちんちゃくにたいおうした。
jp_example_meaning: She responded calmly even in a critical situation.
cn_example: 面对困难，他表现得很沉着。
cn_example_pinyin: Miànduì kùnnán, tā biǎoxiàn de hěn chénzhuó.
cn_example_meaning: Facing difficulties, he remained very calm.
freq_note: Common in descriptions of demeanor and personality
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — formal/literary vocabulary; 冷静 is more commonly used

jp_example: 剛毅な性格で知られている。
jp_example_reading: ごうきなせいかくでしられている。
jp_example_meaning: He is known for his resolute character.
cn_meaning: Resolute and steadfast; firm and unyielding
cn_example_pinyin: Tā de xìnggé gāngyì guǒduàn.
cn_example_meaning: His character is resolute and decisive.
freq_note: Used in literary and formal character descriptions; not everyday vocabulary
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — literary/classical vocabulary

jp_example: 果敢な決断が成功をもたらした。
jp_example_reading: かかんなけつだんがせいこうをもたらした。
jp_example_meaning: A bold decision brought about success.
cn_meaning: Courageous and resolute; bold and decisive
cn_example_pinyin: Tā guǒgǎn de zuò chū le juédìng.
cn_example_meaning: He boldly made the decision.
freq_note: Used in formal and literary contexts; 勇敢 is more common in daily speech
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — literary/formal vocabulary

jp_example: 彼は上司に従順に従った。
jp_example_reading: かれはじょうしにじゅうじゅんにしたがった。
jp_example_meaning: He obediently followed his boss.
cn_example: 她从小就很顺从父母的意愿。
cn_example_pinyin: Tā cóng xiǎo jiù hěn shùncóng fùmǔ de yìyuàn.
cn_example_meaning: She has been obedient to her parents' wishes since childhood.
freq_note: Common in descriptions of behavior and personality
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — descriptive vocabulary commonly used in literature

jp_example: 温厚な人柄で皆に好かれている。
jp_example_reading: おんこうなひとがらでみんなにすかれている。
jp_example_meaning: He is liked by everyone for his gentle personality.
cn_meaning: Warm and kind; gentle and generous
cn_example: 他为人温厚，深受大家喜爱。
cn_example_pinyin: Tā wéirén wēnhòu, shēn shòu dàjiā xǐ'ài.
cn_example_meaning: He is warm and kind, and deeply loved by everyone.
freq_note: Used in literary and formal character descriptions; 温和 is more common in daily speech
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — literary vocabulary; 温和 (wēnhé) is the common equivalent

jp_example: 陰湿ないじめは見えにくい。
jp_example_reading: いんしつないじめはみえにくい。
jp_example_meaning: Insidious bullying is hard to see.
cn_example_pinyin: Dìxiàshì yòu yīn yòu shī.
cn_example_meaning: The basement is dark and damp.
meaning_shift_note: JP 陰湿 is primarily used figuratively to describe underhanded, insidious behavior (especially bullying). CN 阴湿 is mainly used literally to describe a damp and dark environment.
freq_note: Used mainly for describing physical environments; the figurative JP usage does not exist in CN
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — descriptive/environmental vocabulary

jp_example: 彼の不満は露骨に表れていた。
jp_example_reading: かれのふまんはろこつにあらわれていた。
jp_example_meaning: His dissatisfaction was blatantly apparent.
cn_example_pinyin: Tā lùgǔ de biǎodá le bùmǎn.
cn_example_meaning: He blatantly expressed his dissatisfaction.
freq_note: Used in written and formal contexts; the literal meaning 'exposed bone' is also present but the figurative 'blatant' usage matches JP
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — formal/literary vocabulary

jp_example: 辛辣な批評が話題になった。
jp_example_reading: しんらつなひひょうがわだいになった。
jp_example_meaning: The scathing criticism became a topic of discussion.
cn_meaning: Spicy; pungent; biting; harsh (of words or criticism)
cn_example_pinyin: Tā de yáncí fēicháng xīnlà.
cn_example_meaning: His words are very biting.
meaning_shift_note: JP 辛辣 is used almost exclusively figuratively for harsh/biting criticism. CN 辛辣 can mean both literally spicy/pungent (food) and figuratively harsh (words), with the literal food meaning being common.
freq_note: Common in both food descriptions (spicy) and figurative criticism contexts
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — literary/descriptive vocabulary

jp_example: 痛快な勝利を収めた。
jp_example_reading: つうかいなしょうりをおさめた。
jp_example_meaning: They achieved an exhilarating victory.
cn_meaning: Refreshing; forthright; to one's heart's content; delightful
cn_example_pinyin: Jīntiān wán de zhēn tòngkuài!
cn_example_meaning: We had a really great time today!
meaning_shift_note: JP 痛快 emphasizes the thrill of a decisive victory or satisfying outcome. CN 痛快 has broader usage meaning refreshing, to one's heart's content, or being forthright/straightforward — it often describes doing something freely and enjoyably.
freq_note: Common in daily speech for expressing enjoyment and satisfaction
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK — common in spoken Chinese but not in HSK syllabus

jp_example: 長時間正座して足が麻痺した。
jp_example_reading: ちょうじかんせいざしてあしがまひした。
jp_example_meaning: My legs went numb from sitting in seiza for a long time.
cn_meaning: Paralysis; numbness; to lull into inaction
cn_example: 不要麻痹大意，安全第一。
cn_example_pinyin: Bùyào mábì dàyì, ānquán dì yī.
cn_example_meaning: Don't let your guard down — safety first.
freq_note: Common in medical and figurative contexts

jp_example: 食中毒にかかって入院した。
jp_example_reading: しょくちゅうどくにかかってにゅういんした。
jp_example_meaning: I was hospitalized due to food poisoning.
cn_example: 他因为一氧化碳中毒被送进医院。
cn_example_pinyin: Tā yīnwèi yīyǎnghuàtàn zhòngdú bèi sòng jìn yīyuàn.
cn_example_meaning: He was sent to the hospital due to carbon monoxide poisoning.
freq_note: Common in medical and news contexts; CN uses 上瘾 for addiction sense
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK 1-6; commonly known through 食物中毒

jp_example: 免疫力を高めることが大切だ。
jp_example_reading: めんえきりょくをたかめることがたいせつだ。
jp_example_meaning: It's important to boost your immunity.
cn_example: 接种疫苗可以增强免疫力。
cn_example_pinyin: Jiēzhòng yìmiáo kěyǐ zēngqiáng miǎnyìlì.
cn_example_meaning: Vaccination can strengthen immunity.
freq_note: Common in medical and health discussions
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK 1-6; widely used in health and science contexts

jp_example: どのような症状がありますか。
jp_example_reading: どのようなしょうじょうがありますか。
jp_example_meaning: What kind of symptoms do you have?
cn_example: 请详细描述一下你的症状。
cn_example_pinyin: Qǐng xiángxì miáoshù yīxià nǐ de zhèngzhuàng.
cn_example_meaning: Please describe your symptoms in detail.
freq_note: Very common in everyday medical and health conversations

jp_example: 医師の診断を受けるべきだ。
jp_example_reading: いしのしんだんをうけるべきだ。
jp_example_meaning: You should get a diagnosis from a doctor.
cn_example_pinyin: Yīshēng zhěnduàn tā huàn le liúgǎn.
cn_example_meaning: The doctor diagnosed him with the flu.
freq_note: Common in medical contexts and health discussions

jp_example: 医師に薬を処方してもらった。
jp_example_reading: いしにくすりをしょほうしてもらった。
jp_example_meaning: I had the doctor prescribe me medicine.
cn_example: 这种药需要医生的处方才能购买。
cn_example_pinyin: Zhè zhǒng yào xūyào yīshēng de chǔfāng cái néng gòumǎi.
cn_example_meaning: This medicine requires a doctor's prescription to purchase.
freq_note: Common in medical and pharmaceutical contexts
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK 1-6; commonly used in medical settings

jp_example: 自宅で療養することにした。
jp_example_reading: じたくでりょうようすることにした。
jp_example_meaning: I decided to recuperate at home.
cn_example: 他在疗养院休养了三个月。
cn_example_pinyin: Tā zài liáoyǎngyuàn xiūyǎng le sān gè yuè.
cn_example_meaning: He recuperated at a sanatorium for three months.
freq_note: Used in medical and health contexts; 疗养院 (sanatorium) is the most common compound
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK 1-6; somewhat literary/formal

jp_example: 慢性的な腰痛に悩まされている。
jp_example_reading: まんせいてきなようつうになやまされている。
jp_example_meaning: I am troubled by chronic back pain.
cn_example_pinyin: Mànxìngbìng xūyào chángqī zhìliáo.
cn_example_meaning: Chronic diseases require long-term treatment.
freq_note: Common in medical and health discussions
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK 1-6; widely understood medical term

jp_example: 急性の腹痛で救急車を呼んだ。
jp_example_reading: きゅうせいのふくつうできゅうきゅうしゃをよんだ。
jp_example_meaning: I called an ambulance due to acute abdominal pain.
cn_example: 他被诊断为急性阑尾炎。
cn_example_pinyin: Tā bèi zhěnduàn wéi jíxìng lánwěiyán.
cn_example_meaning: He was diagnosed with acute appendicitis.
freq_note: Common in medical contexts; frequently paired with disease names
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK 1-6; widely understood medical term

jp_example: 二つの企業が合併して大企業になった。
jp_example_reading: ふたつのきぎょうががっぺいしてだいきぎょうになった。
jp_example_meaning: Two companies merged to become a large corporation.
cn_meaning: To merge; to combine; to amalgamate
cn_example_pinyin: Zhè liǎng jiā gōngsī juédìng hébìng.
cn_example_meaning: These two companies decided to merge.
meaning_shift_note: JP uses 合併 for both corporate mergers and medical complications (合併症). CN 合并 is broader — meaning to combine or merge anything — and does not carry the medical complication sense (CN uses 并发症 for that).
freq_note: Very common — used broadly for combining, merging, and consolidating

jp_example: 権力の濫用は許されない。
jp_example_reading: けんりょくのらんようはゆるされない。
jp_example_meaning: Abuse of power is unforgivable.
cn_meaning: To abuse; to misuse; to use indiscriminately
cn_example_pinyin: Lànyòng zhíquán shì wéifǎ xíngwéi.
cn_example_meaning: Abuse of authority is illegal.
freq_note: Common in legal, political, and news contexts
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK 1-6; common in formal and legal usage

jp_example: 会社の資金を横領した罪で逮捕された。
jp_example_reading: かいしゃのしきんをおうりょうしたつみでたいほされた。
jp_example_meaning: He was arrested for embezzling company funds.
cn_meaning: To embezzle; to misappropriate; to annex
cn_example_pinyin: Tā yīn qīntūn gōngkuǎn bèi pànxíng.
cn_example_meaning: He was sentenced for embezzling public funds.
meaning_shift_note: JP 横領 is a standard legal term for embezzlement. CN does not use 横領; the equivalent is 侵吞 or 贪污. The characters 横領 are not used in modern Chinese.
freq_note: 侵吞 is used in legal/news contexts; 贪污 is more common for corruption
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK 1-6; formal legal vocabulary

jp_meaning: Breach of trust; breach of duty
jp_example: 背任行為で起訴された。
jp_example_reading: はいにんこういできそされた。
jp_example_meaning: He was prosecuted for breach of trust.
cn_example: 渎职罪是一种严重的犯罪行为。
cn_example_pinyin: Dúzhí zuì shì yī zhǒng yánzhòng de fànzuì xíngwéi.
cn_example_meaning: Dereliction of duty is a serious criminal offense.
meaning_shift_note: JP 背任 is a specific legal term for breach of trust (fiduciary duty). CN does not use 背任; the closest equivalent is 渎职 (dereliction of duty) or 背信 (breach of faith).
freq_note: 渎职 is used in legal and political reporting
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK 1-6; formal legal vocabulary

jp_example: 敵を懐柔する作戦を立てた。
jp_example_reading: てきをかいじゅうするさくせんをたてた。
jp_example_meaning: They devised a strategy to win over the enemy.
cn_meaning: To conciliate; to pacify; to win over through gentleness
cn_example: 古代帝王常用怀柔政策来安抚边疆民族。
cn_example_pinyin: Gǔdài dìwáng cháng yòng huáiróu zhèngcè lái ānfǔ biānjiāng mínzú.
cn_example_meaning: Ancient emperors often used conciliation policies to pacify border peoples.
freq_note: Literary/formal; also a place name (怀柔区 in Beijing)
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK 1-6; literary and historical vocabulary

jp_example: 群衆を扇動して暴動を起こした。
jp_example_reading: ぐんしゅうをせんどうしてぼうどうをおこした。
jp_example_meaning: He incited the crowd and caused a riot.
cn_meaning: To incite; to instigate; to fan the flames
cn_example: 煽动民众的情绪是危险的行为。
cn_example_pinyin: Shāndòng mínzhòng de qíngxù shì wēixiǎn de xíngwéi.
cn_example_meaning: Inciting public emotions is a dangerous act.
freq_note: Common in political and news contexts
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK 1-6; used in formal/political discourse

jp_meaning: Ignoring; killing by silence; treating with silent contempt
jp_example: 彼の提案は黙殺された。
jp_example_reading: かれのていあんはもくさつされた。
jp_example_meaning: His proposal was silently ignored.
cn_meaning: To ignore; to kill by silence
cn_example: 对于这件事，媒体选择了默杀。
cn_example_pinyin: Duìyú zhè jiàn shì, méitǐ xuǎnzé le mòshā.
cn_example_meaning: Regarding this matter, the media chose to ignore it.
freq_note: Rare in modern CN; more literary. JP usage is more established.
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK 1-6; literary and rarely used in CN

jp_example: 報復措置として関税を引き上げた。
jp_example_reading: ほうふくそちとしてかんぜいをひきあげた。
jp_example_meaning: Tariffs were raised as a retaliatory measure.
cn_example: 他发誓要报复那些伤害过他的人。
cn_example_pinyin: Tā fāshì yào bàofù nàxiē shānghài guò tā de rén.
cn_example_meaning: He swore to take revenge on those who had hurt him.
freq_note: Very common in news, drama, and daily conversation

jp_example: 敵を威嚇するために軍事演習を行った。
jp_example_reading: てきをいかくするためにぐんじえんしゅうをおこなった。
jp_example_meaning: Military exercises were conducted to intimidate the enemy.
cn_meaning: To intimidate; to threaten; to menace
cn_example: 不要试图用暴力来威吓别人。
cn_example_pinyin: Bùyào shìtú yòng bàolì lái wēihè biérén.
cn_example_meaning: Don't try to intimidate others with violence.
freq_note: Used in formal contexts; 威胁 (wēixié) is more commonly used in daily CN
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK 1-6; related 威胁 is HSK 6

jp_example: 上司の恫喝に屈しなかった。
jp_example_reading: じょうしのどうかつにくっしなかった。
jp_example_meaning: He did not yield to the boss's intimidation.
cn_meaning: To intimidate; to threaten; to terrorize
cn_example: 他用恫吓的方式逼迫对方让步。
cn_example_pinyin: Tā yòng dònghè de fāngshì bīpò duìfāng ràngbù.
cn_example_meaning: He used intimidation to force the other party to concede.
freq_note: Formal/literary in CN; less common than 威胁 or 恐吓
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK 1-6; literary and formal vocabulary

jp_example: 彼は巧みに相手を籠絡した。
jp_example_reading: かれはたくみにあいてをろうらくした。
jp_example_meaning: He skillfully manipulated and won over the other party.
cn_meaning: To win over by trickery; to rope in; to bribe
cn_example: 他试图笼络人心来巩固自己的权力。
cn_example_pinyin: Tā shìtú lǒngluò rénxīn lái gǒnggù zìjǐ de quánlì.
cn_example_meaning: He tried to win people over to consolidate his own power.
freq_note: Used in political and literary contexts; somewhat formal
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK 1-6; literary and political vocabulary

jp_meaning: Toying with; trifling with; being at the mercy of
jp_example: 運命に翻弄される人生だった。
jp_example_reading: うんめいにほんろうされるじんせいだった。
jp_example_meaning: It was a life tossed about by fate.
cn_meaning: To rummage through; to flip through; to toy with
cn_example: 她在翻弄抽屉里的旧照片。
cn_example_pinyin: Tā zài fānnòng chōuti lǐ de jiù zhàopiàn.
cn_example_meaning: She was rummaging through old photos in the drawer.
meaning_shift_note: JP 翻弄 emphasizes being toyed with or tossed about (often by fate/circumstances). CN 翻弄 primarily means to rummage through or flip through physical objects, with less figurative usage.
freq_note: Used in daily CN but with a more literal/physical meaning
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK 1-6; informal daily vocabulary

jp_example: 自由を束縛されることに耐えられない。
jp_example_reading: じゆうをそくばくされることにたえられない。
jp_example_meaning: I can't bear having my freedom restricted.
cn_meaning: To bind; to restrain; restriction; bondage
cn_example_pinyin: Bùyào bèi chuántǒng guānniàn shùfù.
cn_example_meaning: Don't be bound by traditional ideas.
freq_note: Common in daily speech and writing; used both literally and figuratively

jp_example: 戦後、多くの植民地が解放された。
jp_example_reading: せんご、おおくのしょくみんちがかいほうされた。
jp_example_meaning: After the war, many colonies were liberated.
cn_meaning: Liberation; to liberate; to set free
cn_example: 解放双手，让生活更轻松。
cn_example_pinyin: Jiěfàng shuāng shǒu, ràng shēnghuó gèng qīngsōng.
cn_example_meaning: Free your hands and make life easier.
freq_note: Extremely common; used in historical, political, and everyday contexts (解放军, etc.)

jp_example: 大統領が政治犯を赦免した。
jp_example_reading: だいとうりょうがせいじはんをしゃめんした。
jp_example_meaning: The president pardoned the political prisoners.
cn_example: 国王决定赦免所有政治犯。
cn_example_pinyin: Guówáng juédìng shèmiǎn suǒyǒu zhèngzhì fàn.
cn_example_meaning: The king decided to pardon all political prisoners.
freq_note: Formal/legal vocabulary; understood but not used in daily conversation
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK 1-6; formal legal and historical vocabulary

jp_example: いくら弁解しても信じてもらえなかった。
jp_example_reading: いくらべんかいしてもしんじてもらえなかった。
jp_example_meaning: No matter how much I tried to justify myself, nobody believed me.
cn_meaning: To explain; to justify; to offer an excuse
cn_example: 他试图为自己的行为辩解。
cn_example_pinyin: Tā shìtú wèi zìjǐ de xíngwéi biànjiě.
cn_example_meaning: He tried to justify his own actions.
freq_note: Common in daily and formal contexts
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK 1-6; commonly understood

jp_example: 記者会見で弁明の機会を与えられた。
jp_example_reading: きしゃかいけんでべんめいのきかいをあたえられた。
jp_example_meaning: He was given a chance to explain himself at the press conference.
cn_meaning: To clarify through argument; to distinguish clearly
cn_example_pinyin: Tā nǔlì biànmíng shìshí zhēnxiàng.
cn_example_meaning: He worked hard to clarify the truth of the matter.
meaning_shift_note: JP 弁明 focuses on defending or justifying oneself. CN 辩明 emphasizes distinguishing or clarifying facts, not necessarily self-defense.
freq_note: Less common in CN; 辩解 or 澄清 are more frequently used
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK 1-6; literary vocabulary

jp_example: 誤解を解くために釈明した。
jp_example_reading: ごかいをとくためにしゃくめいした。
jp_example_meaning: I gave an explanation to clear up the misunderstanding.
cn_meaning: To clarify; to explain; judicial clarification
cn_example: 法官有义务对诉讼程序进行释明。
cn_example_pinyin: Fǎguān yǒu yìwù duì sùsòng chéngxù jìnxíng shìmíng.
cn_example_meaning: The judge has a duty to clarify litigation procedures.
meaning_shift_note: JP 釈明 is used broadly for explaining or clarifying any misunderstanding. CN 释明 is primarily a legal term for a judge's duty to clarify procedural matters.
freq_note: Primarily a legal term in CN; not used in everyday speech
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK 1-6; specialized legal vocabulary

jp_example: 被告人が法廷で陳述した。
jp_example_reading: ひこくにんがほうていでちんじゅつした。
jp_example_meaning: The defendant made a statement in court.
cn_meaning: To state; to narrate; statement; testimony
cn_example: 请你陈述一下事情的经过。
cn_example_pinyin: Qǐng nǐ chénshù yīxià shìqíng de jīngguò.
cn_example_meaning: Please state the course of events.
freq_note: Common in legal, academic, and formal contexts
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK 1-6; formal and legal vocabulary

jp_meaning: Abusive language; verbal abuse; outrageous remark
jp_example: 彼の暴言が問題になった。
jp_example_reading: かれのぼうげんがもんだいになった。
jp_example_meaning: His abusive remarks became a problem.
cn_meaning: Violent or harsh words; reckless speech
cn_example: 他因为一时的暴言而后悔不已。
cn_example_pinyin: Tā yīnwèi yīshí de bàoyán ér hòuhuǐ bùyǐ.
cn_example_meaning: He deeply regretted his reckless words of the moment.
freq_note: Uncommon in modern CN; literary/classical. Modern CN prefers 恶言 or 粗话
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK 1-6; literary vocabulary rarely used in modern CN

jp_meaning: Slip of the tongue; gaffe; verbal blunder
jp_example: 大臣の失言が辞任につながった。
jp_example_reading: だいじんのしつげんがじにんにつながった。
jp_example_meaning: The minister's gaffe led to his resignation.
cn_meaning: Slip of the tongue; indiscreet remark; to say something inappropriate
cn_example_pinyin: Jiǔ hòu shīyán shì cháng yǒu de shì.
cn_example_meaning: Saying something inappropriate after drinking is common.
freq_note: Used in CN but less frequently than JP; somewhat literary
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK 1-6; classical-flavored vocabulary

jp_example: 虚偽の申告は犯罪になる。
jp_example_reading: きょぎのしんこくははんざいになる。
jp_example_meaning: Filing a false report is a crime.
cn_example: 他的笑容看起来很虚伪。
cn_example_pinyin: Tā de xiàoróng kàn qǐlái hěn xūwěi.
cn_example_meaning: His smile looks very hypocritical.
meaning_shift_note: JP 虚偽 emphasizes factual falsehood (false statements, false reports). CN 虚伪 emphasizes hypocrisy and insincerity in a person's character or behavior.
freq_note: Common in daily CN for describing hypocritical people

jp_example: データを捏造した研究者が問題になった。
jp_example_reading: でーたをねつぞうしたけんきゅうしゃがもんだいになった。
jp_example_meaning: A researcher who fabricated data became a problem.
cn_meaning: To fabricate; to make up; to concoct (falsehoods)
cn_example_pinyin: Tā bèi zhǐkòng niēzào shìshí.
cn_example_meaning: He was accused of fabricating facts.
freq_note: Common in legal and news contexts
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK 1-6; formal/legal vocabulary

jp_example: 富を誇示するような生活は好ましくない。
jp_example_reading: とみをこじするようなせいかつはこのましくない。
jp_example_meaning: A lifestyle that flaunts wealth is not desirable.
cn_meaning: To show off; to flaunt; to display boastfully
cn_example: 他喜欢向别人夸示自己的成就。
cn_example_pinyin: Tā xǐhuān xiàng biérén kuāshì zìjǐ de chéngjiù.
cn_example_meaning: He likes to show off his achievements to others.
freq_note: Less common in modern CN; 炫耀 (xuànyào) is preferred in daily speech
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK 1-6; literary vocabulary

jp_example: 指導者の威信が失われた。
jp_example_reading: しどうしゃのいしんがうしなわれた。
jp_example_meaning: The leader's prestige was lost.
cn_example: 这位领导在群众中很有威信。
cn_example_pinyin: Zhè wèi lǐngdǎo zài qúnzhòng zhōng hěn yǒu wēixìn.
cn_example_meaning: This leader has great prestige among the masses.
freq_note: Common in political and organizational contexts
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK 1-6; formal political vocabulary

jp_example: 名誉教授の称号を授与された。
jp_example_reading: めいよきょうじゅのしょうごうをじゅよされた。
jp_example_meaning: He was awarded the title of professor emeritus.
cn_example: 维护国家的名誉是每个公民的责任。
cn_example_pinyin: Wéihù guójiā de míngyù shì měi gè gōngmín de zérèn.
cn_example_meaning: Defending the nation's honor is every citizen's duty.
freq_note: Very common in formal, legal, and everyday contexts

jp_example: 彼は信頼できる人物だ。
jp_example_reading: かれはしんらいできるじんぶつだ。
jp_example_meaning: He is a trustworthy person.
cn_meaning: Trust; confidence; to have faith in
cn_example: 他是一个值得信赖的朋友。
cn_example_pinyin: Tā shì yī gè zhídé xìnlài de péngyǒu.
cn_example_meaning: He is a trustworthy friend.
freq_note: Very common in daily speech and writing; similar to 信任

jp_example: 国家への忠誠を誓った。
jp_example_reading: こっかへのちゅうせいをちかった。
jp_example_meaning: He swore loyalty to the nation.
cn_example_pinyin: Tā duì gōngsī fēicháng zhōngchéng.
cn_example_meaning: He is very loyal to the company.
freq_note: Common in formal, political, and literary contexts

jp_example: 背信行為は絶対に許せない。
jp_example_reading: はいしんこういはぜったいにゆるせない。
jp_example_meaning: Acts of betrayal are absolutely unforgivable.
cn_meaning: To break faith; to go back on one's word; treachery
cn_example: 背信弃义的人不值得交往。
cn_example_pinyin: Bèixìn qìyì de rén bù zhídé jiāowǎng.
cn_example_meaning: People who break faith and abandon righteousness are not worth associating with.
freq_note: Used in the set phrase 背信弃义; somewhat literary on its own
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK 1-6; classical/literary vocabulary

jp_example: 彼の目には敵意が込められていた。
jp_example_reading: かれのめにはてきいがこめられていた。
jp_example_meaning: His eyes were filled with hostility.
cn_example: 他对陌生人总是充满敌意。
cn_example_pinyin: Tā duì mòshēngrén zǒng shì chōngmǎn díyì.
cn_example_meaning: He is always full of hostility toward strangers.
freq_note: Common in news, literature, and daily speech
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK 1-6; commonly understood through 敌 (enemy)

jp_example: 二人は親密な関係を築いた。
jp_example_reading: ふたりはしんみつなかんけいをきずいた。
jp_example_meaning: The two built an intimate relationship.
cn_example: 他们是一对亲密的朋友。
cn_example_pinyin: Tāmen shì yī duì qīnmì de péngyǒu.
cn_example_meaning: They are a pair of close friends.
freq_note: Very common in daily speech for describing close relationships

jp_example: 引っ越してから友人と疎遠になった。
jp_example_reading: ひっこしてからゆうじんとそえんになった。
jp_example_meaning: After moving, I became estranged from my friends.
cn_meaning: To alienate; to estrange; distant; cold
cn_example: 他们的关系越来越疏远了。
cn_example_pinyin: Tāmen de guānxì yuè lái yuè shūyuǎn le.
cn_example_meaning: Their relationship has become more and more distant.
freq_note: Common in daily speech for describing fading relationships

jp_example: 彼は仕事と家庭の間で葛藤していた。
jp_example_reading: かれはしごととかていのあいだでかっとうしていた。
jp_example_meaning: He was torn between work and family.
cn_meaning: Kudzu vine; a type of climbing plant
cn_example_pinyin: Shān shàng zhǎng mǎn le géténg.
cn_example_meaning: The mountain is covered with kudzu vines.
meaning_shift_note: Major meaning shift: JP 葛藤 means inner conflict or dilemma (figurative). CN 葛藤 literally means kudzu vine and is NOT used figuratively. CN uses 矛盾 or 纠结 for inner conflict.
freq_note: Rare in modern CN; only used literally for the plant. Not a common word.
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK 1-6; botanical term rarely encountered

jp_example: タイヤの摩耗が激しい。
jp_example_reading: たいやのまもうがはげしい。
jp_example_meaning: The tire wear is severe.
cn_example: 零件的磨耗会影响机器的性能。
cn_example_pinyin: Língjiàn de móhào huì yǐngxiǎng jīqì de xìngnéng.
cn_example_meaning: Wear on parts affects the machine's performance.
freq_note: Technical term; CN more commonly uses 磨损 (mósǔn) in daily speech
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK 1-6; technical/engineering vocabulary

jp_example: 鉄が酸化して錆びた。
jp_example_reading: てつがさんかしてさびた。
jp_example_meaning: The iron oxidized and rusted.
cn_example: 铁在潮湿环境中容易氧化生锈。
cn_example_pinyin: Tiě zài cháoshī huánjìng zhōng róngyì yǎnghuà shēngxiù.
cn_example_meaning: Iron easily oxidizes and rusts in a humid environment.
freq_note: Common in science, health (抗氧化 = antioxidant), and daily contexts
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK 1-6; science vocabulary but widely understood

jp_example: 会場は興奮で沸騰した。
jp_example_reading: かいじょうはこうふんでふっとうした。
jp_example_meaning: The venue was in an uproar of excitement.
cn_meaning: Boiling; to boil; seething with excitement
cn_example: 全场观众的热情沸腾了。
cn_example_pinyin: Quán chǎng guānzhòng de rèqíng fèiténg le.
cn_example_meaning: The enthusiasm of the entire audience was boiling over.
freq_note: Common both literally (boiling water) and figuratively (excitement)

jp_example: テロ組織の資産が凍結された。
jp_example_reading: てろそしきのしさんがとうけつされた。
jp_example_meaning: The terrorist organization's assets were frozen.
cn_meaning: To freeze; to freeze (accounts, assets); to suspend
cn_example: 法院下令冻结他的银行账户。
cn_example_pinyin: Fǎyuàn xiàlìng dòngjié tā de yínháng zhànghù.
cn_example_meaning: The court ordered his bank accounts frozen.
freq_note: Common in news, finance, and legal contexts; also used literally
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK 1-6; commonly seen in news and finance

jp_example: 氷河の融解が加速している。
jp_example_reading: ひょうがのゆうかいがかそくしている。
jp_example_meaning: The melting of glaciers is accelerating.
cn_example: 春天到了，冰雪开始融解。
cn_example_pinyin: Chūntiān dào le, bīngxuě kāishǐ róngjiě.
cn_example_meaning: Spring has come, and the ice and snow are beginning to melt.
freq_note: Used in scientific and literary contexts; CN more commonly uses 融化 (rónghuà) in daily speech
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK 1-6; scientific/literary vocabulary

jp_example: ファイルを圧縮して送ってください。
jp_example_reading: ふぁいるをあっしゅくしておくってください。
jp_example_meaning: Please compress the file and send it.
cn_example: 请把文件压缩后再发送。
cn_example_pinyin: Qǐng bǎ wénjiàn yāsuō hòu zài fāsòng.
cn_example_meaning: Please compress the file before sending it.
freq_note: Very common in technology, business, and daily contexts

jp_example: 景気の収縮が懸念されている。
jp_example_reading: けいきのしゅうしゅくがけねんされている。
jp_example_meaning: There are concerns about economic contraction.
cn_example: 经济收缩导致了大量失业。
cn_example_pinyin: Jīngjì shōusuō dǎozhì le dàliàng shīyè.
cn_example_meaning: Economic contraction led to massive unemployment.
freq_note: Common in economics, medicine, and science contexts
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK 1-6; technical but widely understood

jp_example: 核兵器の拡散を防がなければならない。
jp_example_reading: かくへいきのかくさんをふせがなければならない。
jp_example_meaning: We must prevent the proliferation of nuclear weapons.
cn_meaning: To spread; to diffuse; diffusion; proliferation
cn_example: 防止核武器扩散是国际社会的共识。
cn_example_pinyin: Fángzhǐ héwǔqì kuòsàn shì guójì shèhuì de gòngshí.
cn_example_meaning: Preventing nuclear proliferation is a consensus of the international community.
freq_note: Common in news, science, and political contexts
hsk_note: Not in standard HSK 1-6; commonly seen in news reporting
